items.title: German, UK firms continued to dominate EPO patent representation
items.title_zh: 德英律所继续主导EPO专利代理市场
items.title_en: German and UK Firms Retain Dominance in EPO Patent Representation Amid Market Fluidity
items.summary: However our latest rankings – using new data from IP Pilot – reveals an increasingly fluid legal market in Europe
items.ai_summary_zh: 标题显示德国和英国律师事务所继续在欧洲专利局（EPO）的专利代理服务中占据主导地位。原文摘要指出，基于IP Pilot的最新数据排名揭示了欧洲法律服务市场正变得日益流动化。由于正文材料仅为摘要片段，缺乏具体排名数据、律所名称或详细市场分析，仅能确认上述基本事实。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent rankings utilizing new data from IP Pilot indicate that law firms based in Germany and the United Kingdom continue to hold dominant positions in European Patent Office patent representation. Concurrently, the analysis highlights an increasingly fluid legal services market across Europe, suggesting shifting dynamics in professional service provision despite the sustained leadership of established German and UK entities.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note：鉴于正文证据不足，无法提供具体市场策略。需关注IAM Media后续发布的完整排名报告，以获取关于特定律所市场份额变化及欧洲专利代理行业竞争格局的详细数据。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and in-house counsel, these findings confirm the continued centrality of German and UK firms in EPO proceedings while signaling broader structural shifts in the European legal landscape. The reported fluidity suggests that competitive dynamics are evolving, warranting attention to emerging trends in provider selection and regional specialization without assuming immediate changes in established representation patterns.

items.title: Lamborghini reveals the hidden race to protect supercar secrecy
items.title_zh: 兰博基尼揭秘保护超跑机密的设计权争夺战
items.title_en: Lamborghini Legal Head Addresses New Threats to Supercar Design Protection
items.summary: From spy shots and AI-generated images to the EU’s repair clause, Lamborghini’s head of legal is confronting new threats to design protection.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，兰博基尼法务负责人正应对设计保护面临的新威胁，包括间谍照片、AI生成图像以及欧盟的维修条款（repair clause）。文章主要探讨这些新兴因素对超级跑车外观设计保密性及法律保护构成的挑战。
items.ai_summary_en: The head of legal at Lamborghini has highlighted emerging challenges to design protection in the automotive sector. Key concerns include the proliferation of spy shots and AI-generated images, which threaten the secrecy of new vehicle launches. Additionally, the EU's repair clause is identified as a significant legal factor impacting the scope of design rights for spare parts and visible components.
items.ai_insight_zh: 实务观察：需关注欧盟维修条款在具体司法实践中的适用边界，以及AI生成内容对现有外观设计新颖性判断标准的潜在冲击。后续应监测兰博基尼及相关车企在应对此类非传统侵权手段时的具体合规策略与诉讼动向。
items.ai_insight_en: This commentary highlights the intersection of technological disruption and evolving EU design law. For rights holders, understanding how non-traditional disclosures like AI imagery interact with novelty requirements is essential. The mention of the repair clause underscores the need to monitor legislative impacts on component-level protection.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["兰博基尼法务负责人指出间谍照片和AI生成图像构成设计保护新威胁", "欧盟维修条款（repair clause）被视为影响外观设计保护的关键法律因素之一"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Spy shots and AI-generated images are cited as new threats compromising supercar design secrecy.", "The EU repair clause is recognized as a critical legal element affecting design protection scope."]

items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Automotive brands must assess risks from digital leaks and AI content regarding design novelty.", "Practice note: Monitor the application of the EU repair clause to determine its effect on protected spare parts.", "What to monitor: Future enforcement actions or policy updates concerning AI-generated visual content and design rights."]

The EU repair clause is recognized as a critical legal element affecting design protection scope.

items.title: IP system must adapt to survive, say EUIPO leaders
items.title_zh: EUIPO领导层称IP体系必须适应变化以生存
items.title_en: EUIPO Leaders Warn IP System Must Adapt to AI and Online Enforcement Challenges
items.summary: From AI-generated content to online enforcement disputes, Europe’s top IP officials say the legal framework is being tested as never before.
items.ai_summary_zh: 原文摘要显示，欧洲顶级知识产权官员指出，从人工智能生成内容到在线执法纠纷，现有的法律框架正面临前所未有的考验。EUIPO领导层强调IP系统必须适应这些挑战才能生存。由于正文材料仅为摘要片段，未提供具体适应措施或详细政策细节。
items.ai_summary_en: Senior officials at the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) have stated that the existing intellectual property legal framework is facing unprecedented pressure. The challenges stem primarily from the rise of artificial intelligence-generated content and complex disputes regarding online enforcement, necessitating significant adaptation for the system's continued viability.
items.ai_insight_zh: Practice note: 鉴于正文信息有限，目前仅能确认欧盟IP高层对AI和在线执法挑战的关注。后续需观察EUIPO是否发布具体的政策更新、指南或立法建议以应对上述挑战，以及这些动态对商标权利人在数字环境下的维权策略可能产生的影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This commentary highlights high-level recognition within the EUIPO that current regulations may be insufficient for emerging digital realities. For rights holders and practitioners, this signals potential future shifts in policy or guidance. As specific measures are not detailed in this brief excerpt, stakeholders should track subsequent official communications for concrete regulatory responses.

items.title: Nokia withdraws action against Geely in connected cars dispute
items.title_zh: 诺基亚撤回对吉利互联汽车专利诉讼
items.title_en: Nokia Withdraws Munich Action Against Geely in Connected Car SEP Dispute
items.summary: As Munich Regional Court has now confirmed, Nokia has withdrawn a lawsuit against Geely that was due to be heard today.
items.ai_summary_zh: 慕尼黑地方法院确认，诺基亚已撤回原定于今日审理的针对吉利的侵权诉讼。该诉讼涉及用于互联汽车的4G和5G标准必要专利（SEP），此前诺基亚已在慕尼黑法院和统一专利法院（UPC）提起多起相关索赔。此次撤诉意味着这场看似兴起的互联汽车专利战可能尚未全面展开便告终结，同时也暗示在UPC的相关程序可能提前结束。
items.ai_summary_en: The Munich Regional Court has confirmed that Nokia withdrew its patent infringement lawsuit against Geely, which was scheduled for hearing. The dispute centered on standard-essential patents (SEPs) essential to 4G and 5G standards used in connected vehicles. Nokia had previously filed parallel infringement claims in Munich and at the Unified Patent Court (UPC). This withdrawal suggests that the anticipated wave of litigation regarding connected car connectivity may be concluding prematurely, potentially leading to the early termination of related proceedings at the UPC.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and implementers in the automotive sector, this withdrawal highlights the volatility of multi-jurisdictional SEP enforcement strategies. The cessation of proceedings in both national courts and the UPC suggests that commercial negotiations or strategic reassessments may be influencing litigation trajectories more than initial filings indicated. Stakeholders should observe whether this signals a broader trend of settlement over prolonged judicial resolution in the connected vehicle IP landscape.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["诺基亚正式撤回在慕尼黑地方法院针对吉利的专利侵权诉讼", "争议焦点为应用于互联汽车的4G及5G标准必要专利许可问题", "此举可能导致统一专利法院（UPC）的相关平行程序提前终止"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Nokia formally withdrew its infringement action against Geely at the Munich Regional Court prior to the scheduled hearing.", "The litigation involved 4G and 5G standard-essential patents utilized in connected car technologies across multiple jurisdictions.", "This development indicates a potential premature end to parallel proceedings currently pending before the Unified Patent Court."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注SEP诉讼撤诉背后的商业和解信号与策略调整", "强化FRAND合规准备以应对潜在许可谈判压力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor the impact of withdrawn actions on ongoing SEP licensing negotiations and portfolio valuation in the automotive sector.", "Practice note: Track the procedural consequences of national withdrawals on parallel UPC cases to anticipate case management outcomes.", "What to monitor: Observe if similar withdrawals by major SEP holders indicate a shift towards alternative dispute resolution or private settlements."]
core_points_en: Nokia formally withdrew its infringement action against Geely at the Munich Regional Court prior to the scheduled hearing.
The litigation involved 4G and 5G standard-essential patents utilized in connected car technologies across multiple jurisdictions.
This development indicates a potential premature end to parallel proceedings currently pending before the Unified Patent Court.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor the impact of withdrawn actions on ongoing SEP licensing negotiations and portfolio valuation in the automotive sector.
Practice note: Track the procedural consequences of national withdrawals on parallel UPC cases to anticipate case management outcomes.
What to monitor: Observe if similar withdrawals by major SEP holders indicate a shift towards alternative dispute resolution or private settlements.

items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform
items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
items.title_en: Switzerland Reforms Patent Act with Mandatory Searches and Optional Substantive Examination
items.summary: Key changes in the Swiss Patent Act include a mandatory prior-art search, optional full substantive examination, the acceptance of English-language technical documents and a new appeal system. In March 2024, the Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, introducing new features to the Swiss patent system. The Swiss government, as supported by […] The post Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, introducing significant procedural changes. Key amendments include the implementation of a mandatory prior-art search and an optional full substantive examination system. The reform also allows for the acceptance of English-language technical documents and establishes a new appeal mechanism. These measures mark a shift towards enhanced patent quality and legal certainty within the Swiss jurisdiction.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This legislative update alters the procedural landscape for patent registration in Switzerland. Rights holders should note the introduction of mandatory searches and the option for substantive review, which may impact prosecution timelines and grant probabilities. The acceptance of English documentation offers potential administrative efficiencies for international applicants managing European portfolios.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["瑞士联邦议会于2024年3月通过《专利法》部分修订案", "引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制", "接受英文技术文档并建立全新的专利上诉系统"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The revised Swiss Patent Act introduces a mandatory prior-art search requirement for all patent applications filed in Switzerland.", "Applicants may now opt for a full substantive examination, moving beyond the previous formal examination framework.", "The reform permits the submission of English-language technical documents and implements a new appellate structure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["适应强制检索要求，优化前期现有技术分析与预算规划", "善用英文文件政策降低成本，审慎评估实质审查的商业必要性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor how the optional substantive examination affects grant rates and portfolio strength in the Swiss market.", "Practice note: Leverage the new allowance for English-language technical documents to streamline filing procedures where applicable.", "What to monitor: Track the implementation timeline and specific guidelines for the newly established appeal system."]
core_points_en: The revised Swiss Patent Act introduces a mandatory prior-art search requirement for all patent applications filed in Switzerland.
Applicants may now opt for a full substantive examination, moving beyond the previous formal examination framework.
The reform permits the submission of English-language technical documents and implements a new appellate structure.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how the optional substantive examination affects grant rates and portfolio strength in the Swiss market.
Practice note: Leverage the new allowance for English-language technical documents to streamline filing procedures where applicable.
What to monitor: Track the implementation timeline and specific guidelines for the newly established appeal system.

items.title: Official UPC Year in Review – Five highlights from 2025
items.title_zh: UPC官方2025年度回顾：五大亮点解析
items.title_en: UPC Annual Review 2025: Five Key Highlights from the Court's Second Official Report
items.summary: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) recently released its second official Annual Review, a copy of which can be found on the UPC’s website at UPC Annual Revi...
items.ai_summary_zh: Bristows律所发布分析，解读统一专利法院（UPC）发布的2025年官方年度回顾报告。该报告是UPC的第二份年度报告，全面梳理了2025年的机构活动、案件趋势及运营发展。文章从中提炼出五个对UPC用户至关重要的亮点，旨在帮助利益相关者更好地理解UPC的最新动态与司法实践走向，为在欧专利诉讼策略提供参考依据。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has published its second official Annual Review, detailing institutional activity, case trends, and operational developments throughout 2025. Bristows analyzes this report, extracting five specific highlights deemed most relevant for UPC users. This overview provides essential context on the court's evolving practices and statistical performance during its second full year of operation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This annual review serves as a primary reference for understanding the UPC's operational trajectory and judicial tendencies. Rights holders and legal teams should examine these highlights to align their European patent enforcement expectations with the court's actual caseload management and procedural evolution.

items.title: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院就选择性分销体系在线限制发布指引
items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Online Sales Restrictions in Selective Distribution Systems
items.summary: Restrictions in a selective distribution model affecting online channels or discounting are not automatically illegal but need to be carefully assessed ...
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定，品牌方禁止授权零售商在未品牌折扣网站销售不违反竞争法，推翻竞争上诉法庭原判。法院确认此类在线限制需结合法律与经济背景评估损害程度，而非自动违法。此举虽未放松对转售价格维持等核心限制的执法，但强调品牌应建立透明、客观的分销标准以规避诉讼风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal overturned a Competition Appeal Tribunal ruling, holding that Deckers did not breach competition law by preventing an authorised retailer from selling on an unbranded discount website. The judgment confirms that restrictions on online channels within selective distribution systems are not automatically illegal. Instead, they require a careful assessment of legal and economic context to establish a sufficient degree of harm, aligning with established case law such as Coty. While this clarifies the treatment of channel restrictions, it does not signal leniency towards hardcore restrictions like resale price maintenance, which remains subject to strict enforcement by authorities including the European Commission.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口欧洲的品牌商需警惕选择性分销中的在线限制合规性。建议避免直接禁止线上销售或固定价格，转而制定基于品牌形象和防止搭便车的客观准入标准。完善合同条款与证据留存，可有效应对潜在的反垄断调查与民事诉讼，保障供应链稳定。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision provides important clarity for brands operating selective distribution networks in the UK and EU. It reinforces that channel architecture controls can be lawful if justified by brand positioning or network integrity, rather than being treated as per se violations. However, rights holders must distinguish these legitimate quality controls from hardcore anti-competitive practices. Maintaining transparent, objective criteria for distributor selection and online conduct is essential to mitigate litigation risks and ensure compliance with evolving competition law interpretations across jurisdictions.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["在线销售限制非自动违法，需个案评估法律经济背景及损害程度", "上诉法院推翻初审判决，支持品牌方管控未品牌折扣渠道的合规性", "企业应避免价格固定等核心限制，通过透明标准降低反垄断诉讼风险"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Restrictions on online sales channels in selective distribution are not presumed illegal but require contextual analysis of harm.", "The Court of Appeal ruled that banning sales on unbranded discount websites by authorised retailers does not inherently breach competition law.", "Hardcore restrictions such as resale price maintenance and blanket online bans remain strictly prohibited under current enforcement standards."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["制定基于品牌定位的客观分销标准，避免 blanket ban 线上销售", "严禁转售价格维持等核心限制，完善合规文档以应对反垄断审查"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands can defend channel restrictions by demonstrating they protect brand image and prevent free-riding, avoiding automatic illegality findings.", "Practice note: Document transparent, objective criteria for selective distribution models, specifically regarding website standards and third-party marketplace usage.", "What to monitor: Continued enforcement actions against resale price maintenance and online sales restrictions by the European Commission and national competition authorities."]
core_points_en: Restrictions on online sales channels in selective distribution are not presumed illegal but require contextual analysis of harm.
The Court of Appeal ruled that banning sales on unbranded discount websites by authorised retailers does not inherently breach competition law.
Hardcore restrictions such as resale price maintenance and blanket online bans remain strictly prohibited under current enforcement standards.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands can defend channel restrictions by demonstrating they protect brand image and prevent free-riding, avoiding automatic illegality findings.
Practice note: Document transparent, objective criteria for selective distribution models, specifically regarding website standards and third-party marketplace usage.
What to monitor: Continued enforcement actions against resale price maintenance and online sales restrictions by the European Commission and national competition authorities.

items.title: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
items.title_zh: EPO裁定：基于注册的启用属于非技术特征
items.title_en: EPO Board: Registration-Based Device Enablement Is Non-Technical
items.summary: This page appears to be a security verification checkpoint that temporarily blocks access while validating the incoming request. The system implements protective measures to distinguish legitimate users from automated traffic or potential security threats.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案涉及菲利普莫里斯电热吸烟系统专利的异议程序。上诉委员会认定，设备需经互联网主机注册后方可启用的特征（特征1.7），仅反映非技术的商业或行政要求，不贡献于发明的技术特性。即便该要求导致设备运行状态发生物质变化，其底层概念的非技术性质不变。此裁决明确了在评估创造性时，纯粹由商业规则驱动的功能性限制将被排除在技术贡献之外。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision T 1745/23, EPO Technical Board of Appeal 3.5.01 addressed an electrically heated smoking system by Philip Morris. The claimed invention required the device to be enabled only after registration via an Internet application on a host. The Board determined that this registration-based enablement feature constitutes a non-technical business or administrative constraint. Consequently, it does not contribute to the technical character of the invention. The Board clarified that even if implementing such a requirement causes a material change in the device’s operational state, the underlying concept remains non-technical and is excluded from the assessment of inventive step.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请IoT及智能硬件专利时，应避免将单纯的商业逻辑（如付费解锁、注册激活）作为核心技术特征。撰写时需深入挖掘实现这些功能背后的具体技术改进（如通信协议优化、安全加密算法），确保权利要求聚焦于解决技术问题，以防因缺乏技术贡献而在异议或无效程序中被驳回。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the EPO's strict separation between technical features and non-technical business methods under the problem-solution approach. For IoT and smart hardware applicants, linking device functionality to administrative protocols like user registration will not support inventive step arguments. Drafting strategies must focus on specific technical implementations, such as communication protocols or security algorithms, rather than relying on the functional outcome of business rules to establish patentability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性", "实施商业规则导致的设备状态改变不改变其非技术本质", "在评估创造性时，纯商业或行政要求不被视为对技术的贡献"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business or administrative constraint.", "Material changes in device operational state do not confer technical character on administrative requirements.", "Purely commercial rules driving functional limitations are excluded from technical contribution assessments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利", "聚焦实现功能的具体技术改进以确立专利的技术贡献"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: IoT and smart hardware rights holders must distinguish technical solutions from administrative access controls to secure patent protection in Europe.", "Practice note: Claims should focus on specific technical improvements, such as data encryption or protocol optimization, rather than registration-based activation logic.", "What to monitor: Future Board decisions applying this principle to other connected devices where software updates or user authentication drive operational states."]
core_points_en: Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business or administrative constraint.
Material changes in device operational state do not confer technical character on administrative requirements.
Purely commercial rules driving functional limitations are excluded from technical contribution assessments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: IoT and smart hardware rights holders must distinguish technical solutions from administrative access controls to secure patent protection in Europe.
Practice note: Claims should focus on specific technical improvements, such as data encryption or protocol optimization, rather than registration-based activation logic.
What to monitor: Future Board decisions applying this principle to other connected devices where software updates or user authentication drive operational states.

items.title: FRAND battle between Huawei and TP-Link now focused at UK High Court
items.title_zh: 华为与TP-Link FRAND之争聚焦英国高院
items.title_en: Huawei Withdraws UPC and German Claims, Leaving UK FRAND Rate Dispute with TP-Link
items.summary: Huawei has withdrawn its infringement claims against TP-Link at the UPC and Munich Regional Court and only the UK FRAND proceedings remain.
items.ai_summary_zh: 华为已撤回在统一专利法院（UPC）和慕尼黑地方法院对TP-Link的侵权索赔，争议焦点转向英国高院的FRAND费率设定程序。此前，英国法官Richard Meade已裁定临时许可费率。华为于2025年夏季在UPC慕尼黑分部及中国法院提起诉讼，此次撤诉被视为在英国确立临时费率后的逻辑步骤，凸显了英国作为全球FRAND诉讼关键法域的地位。
items.ai_summary_en: Huawei has withdrawn its patent infringement claims against TP-Link at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and the Munich Regional Court. The dispute concerning Wi-Fi 6 technology now centers exclusively on FRAND rate-setting proceedings at the UK High Court. This strategic shift follows a recent determination by Mr. Justice Richard Meade regarding an interim licence rate. Huawei had originally initiated litigation in summer 2025 across multiple jurisdictions, including China. The withdrawal is described as a logical procedural step following the UK court's interim ruling, consolidating the remaining legal contest into the British forum for final rate adjudication.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视英国法院在SEP许可费率裁定中的主导权。面对跨国诉讼，需评估在不同法域的策略协同，避免资源分散。建议密切关注英国判决对全球费率的影响，并加强在FRAND谈判中的合规准备与证据保全，以应对潜在的禁令风险和高额许可费压力。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the continued strategic importance of the UK High Court in global SEP licensing disputes. By withdrawing continental European infringement claims after securing an interim rate determination, the claimant signals a preference for the UK jurisdiction to finalize licensing conditions. For rights holders and implementers, this highlights the efficiency of using UK proceedings to establish baseline rates, potentially reducing the need for parallel enforcement actions in other European jurisdictions once interim benchmarks are established.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华为撤回UPC及德国侵权索赔，战略重心转移至英国FRAND程序", "英国高院已裁定Wi-Fi 6技术临时许可费率，成为争议解决核心", "案件反映SEP持有人倾向于利用英国法院确定全球或区域许可条件"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Huawei withdrew infringement actions at the UPC and Munich Regional Court after the UK High Court set an interim licence rate.", "The cross-border dispute over Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents now focuses solely on UK FRAND rate-setting proceedings.", "Litigation continues in Chinese courts, while the UK forum remains the primary venue for resolving the licensing terms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视英国法院在SEP费率裁定中的全球影响力与策略价值", "优化跨国诉讼布局，避免多法域并行导致的资源浪费与冲突"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders may prioritize UK FRAND proceedings to secure interim rate benchmarks before deciding on parallel enforcement strategies.", "Practice note: Monitor how UK interim rate determinations influence settlement dynamics and the continuation of parallel litigation in other jurisdictions.", "What to monitor: The final outcome of the UK rate-setting proceedings and their potential impact on ongoing cases in China and other markets."]
core_points_en: Huawei withdrew infringement actions at the UPC and Munich Regional Court after the UK High Court set an interim licence rate.
The cross-border dispute over Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents now focuses solely on UK FRAND rate-setting proceedings.
Litigation continues in Chinese courts, while the UK forum remains the primary venue for resolving the licensing terms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders may prioritize UK FRAND proceedings to secure interim rate benchmarks before deciding on parallel enforcement strategies.
Practice note: Monitor how UK interim rate determinations influence settlement dynamics and the continuation of parallel litigation in other jurisdictions.
What to monitor: The final outcome of the UK rate-setting proceedings and their potential impact on ongoing cases in China and other markets.

items.title: Asus signs up to Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool, ending litigation
items.title_zh: 华硕加入Sisvel Wi-Fi专利池并结束诉讼
items.title_en: Asus Licenses Sisvel Wi-Fi Multimode Pool, Settling Disputes with Huawei and Philips
items.summary: Asus and Sisvel pool members end litigation with licence to the standard essential patents in Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool.
items.ai_summary_zh: 华硕已签署Sisvel Wi-Fi多模专利池许可协议，涵盖Wi-Fi 6和7标准必要专利。此举结束了华硕与华为、飞利浦及Wilus之间的未决专利诉讼。华硕加入惠普企业、索尼等现有被许可人名单。Sisvel未披露具体财务条款。该交易标志着Sisvel在巩固其Wi-Fi专利池市场地位方面取得又一进展，同时也为相关跨国企业的知识产权合规与纠纷解决提供了参考案例。
items.ai_summary_en: Taiwanese electronics manufacturer Asus has secured a license to the standard essential patents within Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool. This agreement resolves pending patent litigation between Asus and key pool members, including Huawei, Philips, and Wilus. The pool encompasses SEPs relevant to Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 7 standards. By joining existing licensees such as Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Sony Group Corporation, and ZTE, Asus expands the reach of Sisvel's licensing program. While the financial terms remain undisclosed, the settlement highlights the continued consolidation of SEP licensing frameworks for next-generation connectivity standards in the global market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 此事件表明Sisvel专利池策略有效，企业应评估加入许可以降低诉讼风险。中国出口企业需密切关注Wi-Fi 7 SEP许可趋势，提前进行FTO分析与许可谈判准备，避免陷入高昂的国际专利纠纷，确保供应链稳定与市场准入安全。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the strategic importance of multi-pool licensing solutions for manufacturers deploying Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 7 technologies. For rights holders and implementers alike, the resolution of litigation through pool participation demonstrates a viable pathway to manage SEP exposure without protracted court battles. European and US stakeholders should observe how such settlements influence broader FRAND negotiation dynamics and the adoption rates of pooled licensing models across the consumer electronics sector.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["华硕获Sisvel Wi-Fi多模专利池许可，覆盖Wi-Fi 6/7 SEP", "协议终结华硕与华为、飞利浦及Wilus的未决专利诉讼", "华硕加入惠普、索尼等行列，成为Sisvel新晋主要被许可人"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Asus obtained a license for Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool covering Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 7 standard essential patents.", "The licensing agreement terminates ongoing patent disputes between Asus and pool members Huawei, Philips, and Wilus.", "Asus joins major technology firms like Sony and ZTE as licensees, reinforcing Sisvel's position in the Wi-Fi SEP landscape."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["评估加入Sisvel专利池以规避高额诉讼成本与禁令风险", "针对Wi-Fi 7标准提前布局SEP许可谈判与自由实施分析"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers implementing Wi-Fi 6/7 should evaluate pool licensing efficiency against individual bilateral negotiations to mitigate litigation risks.", "Practice note: Settlements ending active disputes signal market acceptance of specific pool structures, potentially influencing future FRAND benchmarks.", "What to monitor: Further expansions of the Wi-Fi Multimode pool membership and any subsequent licensing announcements by other major electronics vendors."]
core_points_en: Asus obtained a license for Sisvel's Wi-Fi Multimode pool covering Wi-Fi 6 and Wi-Fi 7 standard essential patents.
The licensing agreement terminates ongoing patent disputes between Asus and pool members Huawei, Philips, and Wilus.
Asus joins major technology firms like Sony and ZTE as licensees, reinforcing Sisvel's position in the Wi-Fi SEP landscape.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers implementing Wi-Fi 6/7 should evaluate pool licensing efficiency against individual bilateral negotiations to mitigate litigation risks.
Practice note: Settlements ending active disputes signal market acceptance of specific pool structures, potentially influencing future FRAND benchmarks.
What to monitor: Further expansions of the Wi-Fi Multimode pool membership and any subsequent licensing announcements by other major electronics vendors.

items.title: Copyright royalty rates excessive of economic value may be abusive of dominant position, rules CJEU against Czech CMO
items.title_zh: CJEU：忽视入住率的版权费率过高或构成滥用市场支配地位
items.title_en: CJEU: Copyright Royalties Ignoring Occupancy May Abuse Dominant Position
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院裁定捷克集体管理组织OSA向酒店收取固定版权费未考虑房间入住率，可能违反TFEU第102条构成滥用支配地位。法院强调版权许可费应与被许可人实际获得的经济价值成比例，若行政上可行，忽略入住率等实际使用情况的定价属不公平高价。该裁决要求版权收费机制必须反映真实经济价值，对欧盟内版权许可实践具有深远影响。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU ruled that a Czech collective management organisation (OSA) may have abused its dominant position by charging hotel operators flat-rate copyright fees without accounting for room occupancy or seasonality. The Court held that under Article 102 TFEU, royalty rates must reflect the actual economic value derived from the use of protected works. By ignoring occupancy levels, OSA’s pricing disconnected fees from the commercial benefit realistically obtained by licensees. This decision clarifies that while installing reception equipment constitutes communication to the public, competition law requires licensing schemes to adequately account for actual usage metrics when determining fair remuneration.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧运营酒店、餐饮及娱乐设施的中国企业应重新审查现有版权许可协议。若支付固定费用且未挂钩实际使用情况，可依据此判例主张费率不公并寻求调整。建议评估历史缴费记录，必要时通过谈判或法律途径争取更公平的计费方式，以降低合规成本并规避反垄断风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the intersection between intellectual property rights and EU competition law, specifically regarding the fairness of licensing terms set by entities holding dominant positions. For rights holders and collective management organisations, it signals that administrative convenience cannot override the requirement to link fees to actual economic value. Licensees, particularly in hospitality and entertainment sectors, may leverage this precedent to challenge rigid pricing models that do not correlate with real-world usage patterns, potentially leading to more nuanced and variable licensing structures across the EU.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定忽略入住率的固定版权费率可能构成滥用市场支配地位", "版权许可费必须与被许可人实际获得的经济价值及作品使用情况成比例", "若行政可行，集体管理组织需采用基于实际使用（如入住率）的计费方法"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU rules that failing to account for hotel room occupancy in copyright fee calculations may constitute an abuse of dominance under Article 102 TFEU.", "Royalty schemes must reflect the actual economic value and commercial benefit derived by licensees from the use of protected copyrighted material.", "Flat-rate fees ignoring seasonal closures or non-operation periods are deemed disproportionate to the size of the relevant public."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在欧中企应审查版权许可费是否与实际使用情况挂钩以规避反垄断风险", "可依据CJEU判例主张固定费率不公，通过谈判或诉讼争取合理计费方式"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Hospitality and entertainment operators should review existing copyright licenses to assess if flat-rate fees ignore actual usage metrics like occupancy.", "Practice note: Collective management organisations must ensure royalty calculation methods adequately reflect the economic value actually received by licensees.", "What to monitor: Future national competition authority decisions applying this CJEU standard to other sectors using collective copyright licensing models."]
core_points_en: CJEU rules that failing to account for hotel room occupancy in copyright fee calculations may constitute an abuse of dominance under Article 102 TFEU.
Royalty schemes must reflect the actual economic value and commercial benefit derived by licensees from the use of protected copyrighted material.
Flat-rate fees ignoring seasonal closures or non-operation periods are deemed disproportionate to the size of the relevant public.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Hospitality and entertainment operators should review existing copyright licenses to assess if flat-rate fees ignore actual usage metrics like occupancy.
Practice note: Collective management organisations must ensure royalty calculation methods adequately reflect the economic value actually received by licensees.
What to monitor: Future national competition authority decisions applying this CJEU standard to other sectors using collective copyright licensing models.

items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations
items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
items.title_en: EU Revised TTBER: Enhanced Guidance on Pools, FRAND and Collective Negotiations
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版《技术转移集体豁免条例》(TTBER)及配套指南，将于5月1日生效。新规保留原有安全港结构，但细化了针对专利池、标准必要技术(SEP)许可及集体谈判的合规指导。对于涉及复杂许可模式的企业，需重点评估协议结构以降低反垄断风险，确保符合TFEU第101(3)条要求。
items.ai_summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. The new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the core Article 101(3) TFEU safe harbour structure while providing detailed guidance on modern licensing practices. Key updates address technology pools, licensing negotiation groups (LNGs), data licensing, and standard-essential technology licensing. For rights holders and licensees, compliance now requires careful structuring of complex agreements to mitigate competition law risks beyond mere market share thresholds.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: The updated framework signals that the European Commission expects parties to actively manage competition law risks in sophisticated licensing structures. While the basic block exemption mechanics remain unchanged, the detailed guidance on pools and collective negotiations means that standard template agreements may no longer suffice for high-value or multi-party arrangements. Rights holders should assess whether their current portfolio licences and negotiation protocols align with the new interpretative guidance to ensure continued eligibility for the safe harbour.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["修订版TTBER及指南于2026年5月1日生效，取代2014年框架", "新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要技术许可的详细竞争法指导", "企业需优化复杂许可协议结构以规避反垄断风险并享受安全港保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The revised TTBER retains the familiar safe harbour for technology transfer agreements below market share thresholds without hardcore restrictions.", "New Guidelines provide specific competition law clarity for technology pools, licensing negotiation groups, and standard-essential technology licensing.", "Complex licensing models involving data rights or collective negotiations require structured documentation to reduce antitrust exposure."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新审查SEP许可协议以符合新规反垄断合规要求", "利用新指南优化专利池参与及集体谈判策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies using patent pools or engaging in collective licensing must review agreement structures against the new Guidelines to maintain safe harbour protection.", "Practice note: Drafting teams should incorporate specific compliance checks for LNGs and SEP licensing clauses to address the Commission's updated expectations on competition risk.", "What to monitor: Future enforcement decisions by the EC regarding technology pools and collective negotiation groups will clarify the practical boundaries of the revised Guideline…"]
core_points_en: The revised TTBER retains the familiar safe harbour for technology transfer agreements below market share thresholds without hardcore restrictions.
New Guidelines provide specific competition law clarity for technology pools, licensing negotiation groups, and standard-essential technology licensing.
Complex licensing models involving data rights or collective negotiations require structured documentation to reduce antitrust exposure.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies using patent pools or engaging in collective licensing must review agreement structures against the new Guidelines to maintain safe harbour protection.
Practice note: Drafting teams should incorporate specific compliance checks for LNGs and SEP licensing clauses to address the Commission's updated expectations on competition risk.
What to monitor: Future enforcement decisions by the EC regarding technology pools and collective negotiation groups will clarify the practical boundaries of the revised Guideline…

items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
items.title_en: German Bad Faith Trademark Filings: DPMA Warns of Rising Strategic Abuse Risks
items.summary: Discover how the concept of ‘Bösgläubigkeit’ (bad faith) operates in German law and how to avoid filing or being infringed by bad‑faith filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
items.ai_summary_en: The German Patent and Trade Mark Office (DPMA) has highlighted a significant increase in potentially bad-faith trademark applications since mid-2025. These filings often target signs already used by third parties without formal protection, aiming to exert competitive pressure rather than indicate commercial origin. The DPMA attributes this trend to technological developments, such as AI tools facilitating the identification of unregistered signs, and online marketplace enforcement mechanisms that allow swift blocking of third parties. Under the German Trademark Act (MarkenG), applications filed without genuine intent to use for abusive objectives are excluded from registration or subject to invalidation.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
items.ai_insight_en: This development underscores the increasing scrutiny of applicant intent in German practice, aligning with broader EU trends. Rights holders must recognize that mere registration is no longer an absolute shield against challenges based on bad faith. Proactive portfolio management is essential to mitigate risks associated with strategic filings designed to disrupt market access or extract undue advantages through enforcement mechanisms.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击", "德国商标法规定，无真实使用意图且旨在滥用权利的注册可被驳回或在事后宣告无效", "恶意认定需结合主观意图与客观环境综合评估，德国司法实践深受欧盟法院判例影响"]

items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解
items.title_en: Strategic Planning for Successful IP Mediation Amidst New European ADR Frameworks
items.summary: Mediation is an increasingly attractive option to resolve IP disputes, especially for small and medium-sized enterprises, individuals and universities
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，成为解决IP纠纷的优选。WIPO数据显示2025年案件激增42%，中小企业占比高。欧盟层面，EUIPO扩大调解服务范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解中心。调解由中立专家主持，灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供法院无法给予的商业解决方案，有助于建立双赢局面。
items.ai_summary_en: Mediation offers a confidential, cost-effective alternative to litigation for resolving intellectual property disputes, particularly benefiting SMEs and individuals. The World Intellectual Property Organization reported a 42% increase in administered cases in 2025, with significant participation from non-corporate entities. In Europe, the landscape is expanding with the EUIPO Mediation Centre extending its services to all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs as of June 2025. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court will inaugurate its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026, providing new forums for alternative dispute resolution across jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临IP纠纷时，应优先考虑利用EUIPO及即将成立的UPC调解机制。相比传统诉讼，调解成本更低且能保护商业秘密，尤其适合中小企业。建议提前在合同中加入调解条款，并熟悉WIPO等机构的规则，以灵活应对侵权或许可谈判，避免高昂 litigation 风险。
items.ai_insight_en: The expansion of institutional mediation frameworks in Europe provides rights holders with structured avenues to resolve cross-border IP conflicts efficiently. For portfolio managers and legal teams, integrating these mechanisms can facilitate commercially focused settlements that courts may not offer. Understanding the procedural nuances of WIPO, EUIPO, and upcoming UPC facilities allows parties to leverage confidentiality and flexibility, potentially preserving business relationships while addressing infringement or licensing disagreements without the adversarial nature of traditional litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["调解具高效保密优势，WIPO案件量增42%，中小企参与度高", "EUIPO扩大调解范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解仲裁中心", "调解灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供超越诉讼的商业双赢方案"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["WIPO recorded a 42% rise in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising 59% of involved parties.", "The EUIPO Mediation Centre expanded its scope in June 2025 to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and registered designs.", "The Unified Patent Court will launch its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026, enhancing European ADR options."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优先利用EUIPO及UPC调解机制降低在欧维权成本与风险", "建议在合同中预设调解条款，灵活运用ADR解决跨境IP争议"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Institutional mediation offers confidential, commercially oriented resolutions for IP disputes, reducing reliance on public litigation.", "Practice note: Consider incorporating mandatory mediation clauses in commercial agreements to utilize EUIPO and future UPC dispute resolution services.", "What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre scheduled for June 2026."]
core_points_en: WIPO recorded a 42% rise in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising 59% of involved parties.
The EUIPO Mediation Centre expanded its scope in June 2025 to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and registered designs.
The Unified Patent Court will launch its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in June 2026, enhancing European ADR options.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Institutional mediation offers confidential, commercially oriented resolutions for IP disputes, reducing reliance on public litigation.
Practice note: Consider incorporating mandatory mediation clauses in commercial agreements to utilize EUIPO and future UPC dispute resolution services.
What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre scheduled for June 2026.

items.title: Pro = Active? CJEU Asked To Decide
items.title_zh: CJEU裁决请求：前药是否可独立获SPC保护？
items.title_en: CJEU Asked to Rule on SPC Eligibility for Pro-Drugs of Previously Approved Active Ingredients
items.summary: HGF operates a comprehensive network of intellectual property offices spanning major cities across the United Kingdom, Europe, and Ireland.
items.ai_summary_zh: 爱尔兰律所HGF分析指出，CJEU已受理案件（C-794/25），需裁定已获批活性成分的前药能否独立获得补充保护证书（SPC）。背景涉及武田制药的LDX（右旋安非他命前药）与母药DEX。尽管LDX作为新活性物质获批上市，但法规对前药是否属“同一活性物质”存疑。此裁决将澄清行业争议，解决相关诉讼，明确前药在欧盟的SPC资格标准。
items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has received a new referral (Case C-794/25) regarding whether an approved pro-drug of a previously authorized active pharmaceutical ingredient qualifies for independent Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection. The case centers on lisdexamfetamine (LDX), a pro-drug of dexamphetamine (DEX). Although LDX was granted marketing authorization as a New Active Substance due to significant differences in safety and efficacy profiles, questions remain under Article 10.2.b of the Medicinal Products Directive. The CJEU’s ruling aims to resolve ongoing litigation and provide legal certainty for rights holders seeking SPCs for derivative compounds that are metabolized into known active ingredients.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral addresses critical uncertainty in EU pharmaceutical IP law regarding the intersection of regulatory approval status and SPC entitlement. For rights holders, the distinction between a 'New Active Substance' for regulatory purposes and an 'active ingredient' for SPC purposes is pivotal. A negative ruling could restrict SPC availability for improved formulations of older drugs, while a positive one would support broader protection for innovative derivatives. Stakeholders should assess how this interpretation affects the valuation of late-stage pipeline assets involving pro-drugs and salts.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护", "争议焦点在于前药是否因安全性或疗效显著不同而视为新活性物质", "裁决结果将消除法律不确定性，直接影响欧盟药品专利策略布局"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug with distinct safety or efficacy properties can secure an SPC independently from its parent active substance.", "Article 10.2.b of the Medicinal Products Directive treats derivatives as the same active substance unless they differ significantly in safety or efficacy.", "The outcome will clarify SPC eligibility standards for pro-drugs like lisdexamfetamine, impacting portfolio strategies for modified pharmaceutical compounds."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注前药SPC资格判例，评估在欧研发管线的专利延长期限风险", "强化安全与疗效差异证据链，确保满足新活性物质认定标准"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must evaluate whether their pro-drug portfolios rely on independent SPC protection or are vulnerable to being classified as the same active substa…", "Practice note: When preparing SPC applications for derivatives, ensure robust documentation of significant differences in safety and efficacy to support arguments for distinct act…", "What to monitor: Monitor the CJEU's final judgment in Case C-794/25 for definitive guidance on interpreting Article 10.2.b of the Medicinal Products Directive in the context of SP…"]
core_points_en: The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug with distinct safety or efficacy properties can secure an SPC independently from its parent active substance.
Article 10.2.b of the Medicinal Products Directive treats derivatives as the same active substance unless they differ significantly in safety or efficacy.
The outcome will clarify SPC eligibility standards for pro-drugs like lisdexamfetamine, impacting portfolio strategies for modified pharmaceutical compounds.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must evaluate whether their pro-drug portfolios rely on independent SPC protection or are vulnerable to being classified as the same active substa…
Practice note: When preparing SPC applications for derivatives, ensure robust documentation of significant differences in safety and efficacy to support arguments for distinct act…
What to monitor: Monitor the CJEU's final judgment in Case C-794/25 for definitive guidance on interpreting Article 10.2.b of the Medicinal Products Directive in the context of SP…

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定：临时禁令需证明单一产品侵权
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Single-Product Requirement and Burden of Proof in Clear Aligner Provisional Measures
items.summary: The applicant bears the burden of proof for infringement in provisional measures and must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not (Art. 62 UPCA, R. 211 RoP)
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在2026年5月12日裁决中驳回申请人临时禁令请求。法院强调，申请人须充分证明侵权行为发生的可能性大于不可能性。针对隐形牙套专利，法院认定所有技术特征必须体现在单个产品中，不同阶段的牙套不能组合认定侵权。因申请人仅依赖宣传材料且缺乏具体技术分析，未能满足举证标准，法院未审理有效性等其他抗辩理由。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed a request for provisional measures in patent infringement proceedings concerning clear aligner technology. The Court held that the applicant bears the burden of proving infringement is more likely than not under Article 62 UPCA and Rule 211 RoP. Crucially, the Court construed the method claim to require all features, including opposing counter moments, to be generated by the tooth receiving cavities of a single aligner. Evidence based solely on promotional materials and speculative witness statements, without independent technical assessment of the specific accused product, was deemed insufficient to meet this standard. The decision underscores that multi-stage treatment phases cannot be combined to establish infringement of a single-product claim.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲时，应警惕UPC对‘单一产品’特征的严格解释。在应对临时禁令指控时，需准备详尽的技术比对分析，避免仅依赖营销材料。建议提前进行FTO分析，确保多阶段治疗方案不落入同一专利的单一产品权利要求范围，以规避高额诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies strict evidentiary standards for preliminary relief at the UPC, particularly for multi-stage medical devices. Rights holders must provide detailed, product-specific technical analyses rather than relying on general marketing assertions. Defendants can challenge infringement allegations by highlighting the absence of feature-by-feature correspondence in individual products. The decision reinforces that claim construction focusing on a single physical embodiment prevents aggregating features from different treatment phases to prove liability.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人承担临时禁令侵权举证责任，需证明侵权可能性大于不可能性", "方法权利要求的所有特征必须在单个对齐器中实现，不可跨阶段组合", "仅凭宣传材料和推测性证言不足以确立复杂技术领域的侵权事实"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants bear the full burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.", "Method claims construed as relating to a single product require all features to be present in one appliance, not across stages.", "Promotional materials and speculative assumptions are inadequate for establishing infringement in complex technical fields."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC对单一产品特征比对的严格要求，避免跨阶段组合侵权风险", "完善技术证据链，避免仅凭营销材料应对复杂的专利侵权指控"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders seeking provisional measures must secure independent technical assessments of specific accused products early in proceedings.", "Practice note: Claim construction arguments should focus on whether all claimed features reside within a single physical unit or device iteration.", "What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply the 'single product' requirement to other incremental or multi-stage therapeutic technologies."]
core_points_en: Applicants bear the full burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.
Method claims construed as relating to a single product require all features to be present in one appliance, not across stages.
Promotional materials and speculative assumptions are inadequate for establishing infringement in complex technical fields.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders seeking provisional measures must secure independent technical assessments of specific accused products early in proceedings.
Practice note: Claim construction arguments should focus on whether all claimed features reside within a single physical unit or device iteration.
What to monitor: How other UPC divisions apply the 'single product' requirement to other incremental or multi-stage therapeutic technologies.

items.title: Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires
items.title_zh: Taylor Wessing聘请Eisenführ Speiser合伙人强化德国专利业务
items.title_en: Taylor Wessing Hires Two Partners from Eisenführ Speiser to Strengthen German Patent Practice
items.summary: The two new partners will join Taylor Wessing on 1 June. The firm is thus underlining its commitment to restoring the renowned patent practice to its former strength following the spin-off of a large team in the autumn. Michael Schneider (56) has more than 20 years of experience in patent litigation, particularly in mobile communications […] The post Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 国际律所Taylor Wessing宣布将于6月1日吸纳两名来自Eisenführ Speiser的新合伙人，以重建其德国专利实践团队。此举旨在弥补去年秋季大规模团队分拆后的实力损失，恢复该所在德国专利领域的传统优势。新加入的合伙人Michael Schneider拥有逾20年专利诉讼经验，尤其在移动通信领域具备深厚专长。这一人事变动凸显了律所在竞争激烈的德国市场中巩固其知识产权服务地位的战略意图。
items.ai_summary_en: International law firm Taylor Wessing has announced the appointment of two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser, effective 1 June. This move aims to restore the strength of its German patent practice following a significant team departure in the previous autumn. One of the new hires, Michael Schneider, brings over 20 years of experience in patent matters, with a specific focus on mobile communications technologies.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信企业应关注德国专利诉讼团队变动，特别是具有移动通信背景的资深律师动向。这预示着德国专利诉讼活跃度可能提升，建议提前评估潜在风险，优化在德国市场的IP布局与应诉策略，必要时建立与该类强势律所的合作或预警机制。
items.ai_insight_en: This personnel change highlights ongoing consolidation and restructuring within the German patent legal market. Rights holders and companies operating in Germany should track these shifts to understand changes in available counsel expertise, particularly in high-volume technology sectors like mobile communications.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["Taylor Wessing于6月1日引入两名新合伙人以强化德国专利团队", "此举旨在修复去年团队分拆影响，恢复其在德国市场的领先地位", "新合伙人Michael Schneider深耕移动通信领域二十余年专利诉讼"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Taylor Wessing appoints two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser starting 1 June to rebuild its German patent team.", "The hiring strategy addresses capacity gaps created by a large team spin-off that occurred in the autumn.", "New partner Michael Schneider contributes more than two decades of experience specializing in mobile communications."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注德所人员变动，预判移动通信领域专利诉讼活跃度上升", "提前优化在欧IP布局，建立针对资深诉讼专家的应对与合作机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Monitor changes in firm capacity and specialist expertise in key technology sectors for future engagement planning.", "Practice note: Track lateral hiring trends among major IP firms to assess market stability and service availability in Germany.", "What to monitor: Further developments in the composition of leading German patent practices and their impact on sector-specific legal services."]
core_points_en: Taylor Wessing appoints two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser starting 1 June to rebuild its German patent team.
The hiring strategy addresses capacity gaps created by a large team spin-off that occurred in the autumn.
New partner Michael Schneider contributes more than two decades of experience specializing in mobile communications.

Practice note: Track lateral hiring trends among major IP firms to assess market stability and service availability in Germany.
What to monitor: Further developments in the composition of leading German patent practices and their impact on sector-specific legal services.

items.title: Legal holds and document preservation: How to get on the front foot
items.title_zh: 法律保留与文件保全：如何掌握诉讼主动权
items.title_en: UK Legal Holds: Mandatory Document Preservation Under PD57AD
items.summary: Identification and preservation of documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute isn’t just best practice, it is a mandatory procedural requirem...
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，在争议早期识别并保存文件证据不仅是最佳实践，更是强制性程序要求。依据《民事诉讼规则》及PD57AD，当当事人知晓可能成为诉讼方时，必须立即采取具体步骤识别并保存相关文件。这包括实施“停止时钟”的数据保留措施。及时且妥善的法律保留能确保在后续收集和审查阶段前完成初步工作，从而在诉讼中占据有利地位，避免因证据灭失而面临不利后果。
items.ai_summary_en: Under English law, identifying and preserving documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute is a mandatory procedural requirement, not merely best practice. Practice Direction 57AD to the Civil Procedure Rules imposes strict obligations on parties who know they may become involved in litigation. This includes immediate 'stop the clock' measures to prevent data alteration or deletion. The definition of 'document' is broad, covering anything in which information is recorded. Properly implemented legal holds ensure compliance with disclosure duties and facilitate subsequent evidence collection and review processes.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对欧洲IP纠纷时，应建立即时响应机制。一旦收到律师函或知悉潜在诉讼，须立即冻结相关电子数据与文档，防止自动覆盖。忽视此程序义务可能导致证据被排除或面临制裁。建议提前制定合规预案，确保在UPC或英国法院诉讼中有效履行披露义务，维护自身合法权益。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and companies operating in the UK, understanding the strict timing of preservation duties is critical for effective portfolio management and dispute readiness. Failure to comply with these procedural requirements can lead to significant adverse consequences during litigation. Establishing robust internal protocols ensures that evidence remains intact and admissible, supporting stronger positions in enforcement or defense scenarios without relying on specific litigation tactics.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法律保留是强制性程序义务，非仅最佳实践，需严格遵守", "触发点为知晓可能成为诉讼当事人，须立即启动文件识别与保存", "需执行“停止时钟”数据保留，确保早期保全以利于后续举证"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["PD57AD triggers preservation duties when a party knows it may become involved in proceedings.", "Parties must implement immediate 'stop the clock' measures to preserve relevant electronic data.", "The scope of 'document' for disclosure purposes includes any medium recording information."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立即时响应机制，知悉潜在诉讼即冻结相关电子数据", "重视程序合规，避免因未履行证据保全义务导致证据失效"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Early identification of potential disputes allows for timely compliance with mandatory preservation duties under English procedural rules.", "Practice note: Implement automated 'stop the clock' mechanisms to secure electronic data immediately upon recognizing litigation risk.", "What to monitor: Updates to Practice Direction 57AD and Civil Procedure Rules regarding the scope and technical standards for document preservation."]
core_points_en: PD57AD triggers preservation duties when a party knows it may become involved in proceedings.
Parties must implement immediate 'stop the clock' measures to preserve relevant electronic data.
The scope of 'document' for disclosure purposes includes any medium recording information.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Early identification of potential disputes allows for timely compliance with mandatory preservation duties under English procedural rules.
Practice note: Implement automated 'stop the clock' mechanisms to secure electronic data immediately upon recognizing litigation risk.
What to monitor: Updates to Practice Direction 57AD and Civil Procedure Rules regarding the scope and technical standards for document preservation.

items.title: Ban on the use of meat names for plant-based alternatives
items.title_zh: 欧盟达成禁令：植物基替代品禁用肉类名称
items.title_en: EU Agreement Bans Specific Meat Names for Plant-Based Alternatives
items.summary: The EU recently reached an agreement on the question of whether a meat name could be used for plant-based veg products marketed as a substitute for meat.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟机构达成协议，禁止在植物基替代产品上使用特定肉类名称（如鸡肉、牛肉），旨在防止消费者被误导并依据《欧盟商标条例》第7条避免注册误导性商标。通用词汇如“汉堡”仍可使用。该禁令可能导致含肉类名称的植物基品牌无法继续使用现有注册商标，迫使供应商修改包装及品牌策略以符合新规。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Parliament, Council, and member states have agreed to prohibit the use of specific meat names for plant-based alternatives. This measure aims to prevent consumer confusion by reserving terms like 'chicken' or 'beef' exclusively for animal products. While generic descriptors such as 'burger' remain permissible, brands containing restricted animal references may face usage restrictions under Article 7 of the EU Trademark Regulation, which prohibits misleading trademarks regarding product nature.
items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧出口植物基食品的中国企业而言，需立即审查现有商标及包装，移除鸡、牛等具体肉类词汇以避免侵权或违规。建议保留burger等通用词，重新设计品牌标识。同时评估现有注册商标的有效性，必要时进行变更或新申请，确保符合欧盟反误导规定，降低法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This regulatory shift impacts portfolio management for food sector rights holders. Brands using protected marks with specific animal references must assess compliance risks, as legal protection does not automatically override statutory bans on misleading commercial practices. Companies should evaluate whether their current packaging and branding strategies align with the new distinction between specific meat terms and generic descriptors.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟禁止植物基产品使用特定动物部位名称，以防误导公众", "依据EUTMR第7条，误导性商标不得注册，现有品牌可能受限", "通用描述词如burger允许使用，但需移除具体肉类指代词汇"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Specific animal part terms including chicken, beef, and bacon are prohibited for vegetarian products to ensure clarity.", "Generic descriptive words like burger or sausage remain allowed for marketing plant-based food substitutes.", "Existing trademarks referencing specific animals may become unusable if deemed misleading under current EU regulations."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即审查在欧植物基产品包装，移除具体肉类名称以合规", "评估现有含肉类词商标风险，及时调整品牌策略应对禁令"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders in the plant-based sector must review existing trademarks and packaging for prohibited specific meat terminology.", "Practice note: Assess potential conflicts between registered trademark rights and the statutory ban on misleading indications under EUTMR Article 7.", "What to monitor: Implementation timelines and enforcement guidance from national authorities regarding the removal of restricted terms from labels."]
core_points_en: Specific animal part terms including chicken, beef, and bacon are prohibited for vegetarian products to ensure clarity.
Generic descriptive words like burger or sausage remain allowed for marketing plant-based food substitutes.
Existing trademarks referencing specific animals may become unusable if deemed misleading under current EU regulations.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders in the plant-based sector must review existing trademarks and packaging for prohibited specific meat terminology.
Practice note: Assess potential conflicts between registered trademark rights and the statutory ban on misleading indications under EUTMR Article 7.
What to monitor: Implementation timelines and enforcement guidance from national authorities regarding the removal of restricted terms from labels.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 30, 2026, Order Rejecting Appeal As Inadmissible, UPC_CoA_1/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定驳回上诉：程序合规与费用担保标准
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Rejects Adobe Appeal on Cost Security, Upholds Strict Procedural Compliance
items.summary: The Court of Appeal verifies ex officio compliance with the time limits and rules governing the appeal. When deciding on the admissibility of an appeal, an order rejecting the appeal as...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在Adobe诉Keeex案中，裁定驳回上诉并确认程序规则需严格适用以保障法律确定性。法院依职权审查上诉期限，认定驳回裁定未超出请求范围。此外，法院重申原告与被告的相对财务状况不构成Rule 158下的费用担保考量因素。该判决强化了UPC程序规则的刚性，强调避免司法任意性，对后续涉及费用担保及程序合规的案件具有先例约束力。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal issued an order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible in proceedings between Adobe Inc. and Keeex SAS concerning cost security. The Court confirmed it verifies compliance with time limits and procedural rules ex officio. It held that rejecting an appeal as inadmissible does not exceed the scope of the claims. Furthermore, the Court reaffirmed that strict application of procedural rules ensures legal certainty and prevents arbitrary treatment. Crucially, the relative financial situation of the claimant compared to the defendant is not a valid criterion for assessing cost security under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，须严守程序期限，避免因技术性违规导致权利丧失。针对费用担保，不能仅凭双方财力差距抗辩，需准备实质性的胜诉可能性证据。建议加强内部IP合规流程，特别是跨境诉讼中的程序管理，以降低因程序失误带来的高额成本风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision reinforces the UPC's commitment to rigid procedural adherence to maintain legal certainty and equal treatment. For rights holders and defendants, it clarifies that financial arguments alone cannot override standard cost security requirements. Practitioners must ensure precise compliance with filing deadlines and procedural formalities, as the Court will actively enforce these rules without leniency based on party status or financial standing.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院依职权严格审查上诉期限与程序规则，确保法律确定性及非歧视原则", "驳回上诉作为不可受理裁定，不视为超出当事人诉讼请求的范围", "当事人相对财务状况不构成申请费用担保的法定依据，维持既往判例立场"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court of Appeal conducts an ex officio assessment of admissibility, verifying strict compliance with time limits and procedural rules governing appeals.", "An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is deemed within the scope of the claims and does not violate procedural boundaries under the Rules of Procedure.", "The relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant does not constitute a valid criterion for cost security assessments under Rule 158."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守UPC程序期限，规避因技术性违规导致的权利丧失风险", "费用担保抗辩需侧重胜诉可能性，而非单纯依赖财力对比"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Financial disparity between parties is insufficient to challenge cost security orders; substantive procedural compliance is paramount.", "Practice note: Ensure rigorous internal monitoring of appeal deadlines and procedural steps, as the Court reviews admissibility ex officio.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 regarding cost security criteria and any potential shifts in the Court's strict interpretation of procedural rules."]
core_points_en: The Court of Appeal conducts an ex officio assessment of admissibility, verifying strict compliance with time limits and procedural rules governing appeals.
An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is deemed within the scope of the claims and does not violate procedural boundaries under the Rules of Procedure.
The relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant does not constitute a valid criterion for cost security assessments under Rule 158.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Financial disparity between parties is insufficient to challenge cost security orders; substantive procedural compliance is paramount.
Practice note: Ensure rigorous internal monitoring of appeal deadlines and procedural steps, as the Court reviews admissibility ex officio.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 regarding cost security criteria and any potential shifts in the Court's strict interpretation of procedural rules.

items.title: Réplique de Ferrari F355 : une contrefaçon sanctionnée par la justice néerlandaise
items.title_zh: 荷兰法院裁定法拉利F355仿制车侵犯版权及商标
items.title_en: Dutch Court Rules Ferrari F355 Replica Infringes Copyright and Trademark Rights
items.summary: Le tribunal de La Haye a sanctionné la commercialisation d'une réplique de Ferrari F355 pour contrefaçon de marque et atteinte au droit d'auteur. Cette décision du 8 avril 2026 illustre la protection cumulative offerte...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月8日，海牙地方法院裁定一荷兰车商销售的丰田改装法拉利F355仿制车侵权。法院认定该车型整体设计具独创性，受版权保护；且使用法拉利Logo构成商标侵权。被告需停止销售、销毁车辆并赔偿。此案凸显了欧盟法域内版权与商标权的累积保护机制，即使存在细微差异，若整体印象相似仍可能被判侵权。
items.ai_summary_en: The District Court of The Hague ruled on April 8, 2026, that a Dutch car dealer infringed Ferrari's copyright and trademark rights by selling a Toyota-based replica marketed as a Ferrari F355. The court found the vehicle's overall design sufficiently original for copyright protection, dismissing minor technical differences as negligible given the identical overall impression. Additionally, the unauthorized use of Ferrari logos constituted EU trademark infringement. The defendant was ordered to cease sales, destroy the replica, pay legal costs exceeding €22,000, and provide further compensation. This judgment highlights the cumulative protection available under EU law for automotive designs.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国车企出口欧洲需警惕外观设计的版权风险，不仅限于专利。改装或复刻经典车型时，即便更换核心部件，若整体视觉印象相似且使用原品牌标识，将面临高额赔偿及销毁风险。建议提前进行FTO分析，避免在营销中使用易混淆的品牌元素。
items.ai_insight_en: This case illustrates the robust cumulative protection afforded to automotive designs in the Netherlands and broader EU jurisdiction. Rights holders can leverage both copyright and trademark laws to combat replicas that mimic the overall aesthetic of protected models, even if internal components differ. For market participants dealing in modified or classic cars, reliance on technical differences alone is insufficient to avoid liability if the visual identity remains confusingly similar to the original brand.

items.title: EPO Enlarged Board Clarifies: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art, Even If Irreproducible
items.title_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会：公开产品即便不可再现亦属现有技术
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art Even If Irreproducible
items.summary: The European Patent Office's Enlarged Board has fundamentally altered the landscape of prior art assessment, establishing that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art regardless of whether they can be analyzed or reproduced. This landmark decision removes the long-standing reproducibility requirement, making market availability alone sufficient to destroy novelty and significantly raising the stakes for timely patent filings.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年7月2日，EPO扩大上诉委员会在G1/23案中裁定，申请日前公开的产品构成现有技术，无论其成分是否可被分析或再现。该决定推翻了G1/92案确立的“可再现性”要求，认为仅市场可获得性即足以破坏新颖性。此举消除了长期存在的法律不确定性，显著提高了及时提交专利申请的重要性，任何上市前公开均可能使后续专利无效。
items.ai_summary_en: In decision G 1/23, the EPO Enlarged Board of Appeal ruled that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art under Article 54(2) EPC, regardless of whether their composition or internal structure can be analyzed or reproduced. This judgment departs from the earlier G 1/92 precedent, which required both public availability and reproducibility for a product to form part of the state of the art. The Enlarged Board found that maintaining a reproducibility requirement would lead to absurd results, as many materials rely on irreproducible starting elements. Consequently, market availability alone is now sufficient to destroy novelty, significantly expanding the scope of prior art.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕产品上市前的任何公开行为，包括展会展示或销售，因即使技术细节未完全披露，产品本身也可作为现有技术驳回专利。建议建立严格的保密与申请时间管理机制，确保在申请日前不公开核心技术，避免在新规下丧失新颖性。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling fundamentally alters the landscape of prior art assessment in Europe. Rights holders must recognize that any public disclosure, including sales or exhibitions, may invalidate subsequent patent applications if the product itself is considered prior art, irrespective of reverse-engineering capabilities. This heightens the importance of strict confidentiality measures and timely filing strategies to prevent inadvertent loss of novelty due to pre-filing market activities.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推翻G1/92先例，取消现有技术认定中的“可再现性”硬性门槛", "确立市场公开即构成现有技术原则，无论内部结构能否被反向工程", "极大扩展现有技术范围，对专利新颖性审查及申请策略产生深远影响"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.", "Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.", "The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化申请前保密管理，严防产品上市导致新颖性丧失风险", "调整全球布局策略，优先在欧洲完成申请后再进行市场推广"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.", "Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.", "What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings."]
core_points_en: G 1/23 establishes that public availability alone suffices for prior art status, removing the reproducibility requirement previously mandated by G 1/92.
Products placed on the market before the filing date are prior art even if their internal structure or composition cannot be analyzed without undue burden.
The decision expands the state of the art to include all publicly accessible products, impacting novelty assessments for chemical and material compositions.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Market availability now definitively destroys novelty, requiring rigorous control over pre-filing disclosures and product launches.
Practice note: Assess prior art risks based on physical availability rather than analytical accessibility when evaluating novelty against existing products.
What to monitor: Future case law applying G 1/23 to specific technical fields and potential impacts on opposition and invalidation proceedings.

items.title: PMAC Advances Operations with New Procedural Rules and Launch of Case Management System
items.title_zh: UPC专利调解仲裁中心发布新规并上线案件管理系统
items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Adopts Rules and Launches Case Management System
items.summary: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has taken significant steps toward full operational readiness,
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会于2026年4月正式通过专利调解和仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则，并于5月12日上线专用案件管理系统（CCMS）。PMAC旨在提供调解、仲裁等替代性争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利及标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷。此举标志着PMAC具备全面运营能力，为权利人提供了高效灵活的专利争议解决新途径，进一步完善了UPC生态系统。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has achieved key operational milestones with the adoption of its procedural rules and the launch of its Case Management System. The Administrative Committee formally approved the framework on 24 April 2026, establishing the legal basis for offering mediation, arbitration, and expert determination services. These mechanisms cover European patents, Unitary Patents, and Standard Essential Patent disputes. The electronic Case Management System went live on 12 May 2026, enabling digital submission of ADR requests. Mediation services are scheduled to begin in late May 2026, with other services following later in the year.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉及专利纠纷时，可关注UPC-PMAC提供的调解与仲裁渠道。相比传统诉讼，ADR程序更具灵活性与保密性，有助于降低维权成本并加速SEP许可谈判进程。建议企业评估将部分非核心或复杂跨国争议转入PMAC处理，以优化全球知识产权战略布局。
items.ai_insight_en: The operational launch of the UPC’s Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre provides rights holders with structured alternative dispute resolution options alongside traditional litigation. This development is particularly relevant for managing complex patent portfolios and resolving high-stakes technical or licensing disputes efficiently. Parties should review the newly published procedural rules and fee schedules to understand the scope and requirements of these non-litigation pathways within the unified patent system.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC行政委员会正式批准PMAC程序规则，奠定法律与运营基础", "PMAC案件管理系统正式上线，支持电子提交替代性争议解决请求", "服务范围涵盖欧洲专利及SEP/FRAND案件，提供多元ADR选项"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UPC Administrative Committee adopted PMAC's procedural rules on 24 April 2026, creating the legal foundation for alternative dispute resolution services within the court ecosy…", "PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System on 12 May 2026, allowing parties to electronically submit requests for mediation, arbitration, and expert determination proceedi…", "The Centre will handle disputes involving European and Unitary Patents, including cross-border conflicts and Standard Essential Patent matters, with mediation services commencing…"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC的ADR机制可降低在欧专利诉讼成本并提升解决效率", "针对SEP/FRAND纠纷，可探索通过调解仲裁达成更优许可条件"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Offers a new avenue for resolving SEP/FRAND and cross-border patent disputes outside of full litigation, potentially enhancing flexibility in portfolio managemen…", "Practice note: Review the adopted procedural rules and CCMS guidance to prepare for electronic case submissions as mediation services commence in late May 2026.", "What to monitor: Track the rollout schedule for arbitration and expert determination services later in the year and any updates to applicable fee structures."]
