items.title: Keeping your word
items.title_zh: 保持信誉：文字商标用于Logo仍视为真实使用
items.title_en: UKIPO: Using Registered Word Marks in Logos Can Constitute Genuine Use
items.summary: Using a registered word mark as part of a logo mark does not preclude a finding of genuine use – as long as the words remain the dominant element, writes Trecina Sookhoo. O/0080/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Limo UK Ltd; O/0028/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v EasyTerra Besloten Vennootschap; O/1148/25, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Avenues Ltd, UK IPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国知识产权局（UKIPO）近期三起案件（O/0080/26等），探讨将注册文字商标作为图形标识一部分使用的法律效力。文章指出，只要文字元素在整体标识中保持主导地位，这种使用方式并不妨碍被认定为“真实使用”。该裁决澄清了商标使用形式的合规性标准，为权利人在品牌视觉升级中保留法律保护提供了依据，强调了维持商标显著特征的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent UK Intellectual Property Office decisions confirm that incorporating a registered word mark into a composite logo does not preclude a finding of genuine use. Provided the word element remains the dominant feature of the overall sign, such usage satisfies the requirement to maintain registration validity. This analysis covers three specific opposition cases involving Kilburn & Strode LLP and various respondents, clarifying the standards for acceptable trademark use forms under current UK practice.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英欧市场进行品牌视觉升级时，常将文字与图形结合。需注意保留文字的主导地位，避免因过度设计导致被视为新标识而丧失原有保护。建议定期审查商标使用证据，确保符合“真实使用”标准，以防因连续五年不使用而被撤销注册，保障品牌资产安全。
items.ai_insight_en: This development provides clarity for portfolio management regarding brand evolution. Companies updating their visual identity can integrate existing word marks into new logos without necessarily jeopardizing their registration status, provided the core distinctiveness is preserved. Legal teams should review evidence of use to ensure it aligns with these dominance criteria to defend against non-use cancellation actions effectively.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["文字商标融入图形Logo若保持主导元素，可认定构成真实使用", "UKIPO在多起案件中确认非纯粹文字形式的使用符合维持注册要求", "权利人需确保修改后的标识未改变原注册商标的显著特征"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Incorporating a registered word mark into a logo design is permissible for genuine use purposes if the words remain the dominant element.", "The UK IPO confirmed this principle across three recent decisions, rejecting arguments that composite use invalidates the original word mark protection.", "Rights holders must ensure that visual modifications do not alter the distinctive character of the originally registered word mark."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["品牌视觉升级应保留文字主导地位以维持注册有效性", "定期审查使用证据以防因未真实使用导致商标被撤销"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands evolving visual identities can retain protection for word marks within composite logos if dominance is maintained.", "Practice note: Audit trademark use evidence to confirm that word elements remain visually dominant in current marketing materials.", "What to monitor: Future UK IPO or court rulings defining 'dominant element' thresholds in composite trademark disputes."]
core_points_en: Incorporating a registered word mark into a logo design is permissible for genuine use purposes if the words remain the dominant element.
The UK IPO confirmed this principle across three recent decisions, rejecting arguments that composite use invalidates the original word mark protection.
Rights holders must ensure that visual modifications do not alter the distinctive character of the originally registered word mark.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands evolving visual identities can retain protection for word marks within composite logos if dominance is maintained.
Practice note: Audit trademark use evidence to confirm that word elements remain visually dominant in current marketing materials.
What to monitor: Future UK IPO or court rulings defining 'dominant element' thresholds in composite trademark disputes.

items.title: The distinguishing mark of man
items.title_zh: 名人之名非版权保护对象：奥威尔遗产诉EUIPO案评析
items.title_en: Copyright Protects Works, Not Names: EUIPO Rejects Orwell Estate Claim
items.summary: No matter how famous an author is, copyright exists to protect their work, not their name, writes Matthew Harris. R 2248/2019-G, The Estate of the Late Sonia Brownell Orwell v EUIPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评析了EUIPO上诉委员会在R 2248/2019-G案中的决定，涉及已故作家乔治·奥威尔的遗产管理人Sonia Brownell Orwell。文章指出，尽管作者享有盛誉，但版权法的宗旨在于保护其创作的作品，而非其姓名或名声。该案件凸显了在欧盟商标注册中，试图通过版权主张来垄断知名人物姓名的法律局限性，强调了知识产权不同领域间的界限。
items.ai_summary_en: This commentary by Matthew Harris for CITMA analyzes the EUIPO Board of Appeal decision in case R 2248/2019-G, involving The Estate of the Late Sonia Brownell Orwell. The core legal principle reaffirmed is that copyright law exists to protect an author's creative works, not their name or personal reputation, regardless of the author's fame. The analysis highlights the limitations of attempting to secure trademark protection for a famous name solely through copyright assertions, emphasizing the distinct boundaries between different intellectual property rights within the EU framework.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局品牌时，应明确版权与商标权的边界。切勿试图利用版权法垄断通用名称或名人姓名以获取商标独占权，此举在欧盟缺乏法律依据。建议企业建立清晰的IP组合策略，分别通过商标和版权保护不同的资产要素，避免因权利基础混淆导致注册失败或无效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and applicants operating in Europe, this decision underscores the importance of selecting the correct legal basis for IP protection. While copyright safeguards literary and artistic expressions, it does not extend to exclusive control over a person's name. Applicants seeking to register marks associated with famous figures must rely on appropriate trademark grounds rather than copyright arguments, ensuring compliance with established EU jurisprudence regarding the separation of IP regimes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["版权法旨在保护作品表达，不延伸至保护作者的姓名或公众声誉", "EUIPO驳回奥威尔遗产方基于版权主张注册其姓名的尝试", "知名人物姓名不能仅凭版权理由获得排他性商标保护"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright law protects the expression of creative works rather than the names or reputations of authors.", "The EUIPO rejected attempts by the Orwell Estate to register a famous name based on copyright claims.", "Intellectual property rights have distinct scopes, preventing copyright from monopolizing personal names."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["厘清版权与商标保护客体差异，避免权利基础混淆", "在欧注册含名人元素商标需评估版权主张的法律可行性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must distinguish between copyrightable works and non-copyrightable elements like names when building portfolios.", "Practice note: Trademark applications relying on copyright assertions for famous names face significant legal hurdles at the EUIPO.", "What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions clarifying the boundary between copyright protection and trademark registration of personal identifiers."]
core_points_en: Copyright law protects the expression of creative works rather than the names or reputations of authors.
The EUIPO rejected attempts by the Orwell Estate to register a famous name based on copyright claims.
Intellectual property rights have distinct scopes, preventing copyright from monopolizing personal names.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must distinguish between copyrightable works and non-copyrightable elements like names when building portfolios.
Practice note: Trademark applications relying on copyright assertions for famous names face significant legal hurdles at the EUIPO.
What to monitor: Future EUIPO decisions clarifying the boundary between copyright protection and trademark registration of personal identifiers.

items.title: How to file an opposition at the European Patent Office (EPO) | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 如何在欧洲专利局提起专利异议
items.title_en: Procedural Requirements for Filing EPO Oppositions Under Article 100 EPC
items.summary: An opposition is a central way for third parties to challenge a granted European patent.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文详解在欧洲专利局（EPO）对已授权专利提起异议的程序。异议是第三方挑战专利有效性的核心途径，须在法定期限内提交书面或电子申请并缴费。异议理由严格限于《欧洲专利公约》第100条规定的缺乏可专利性、公开不充分或超范围等特定法律依据。文章强调需通过官方渠道如在线 filing 2.0 提交，且费用支付需即时完成，为从业者提供实操指引。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis outlines the procedural framework for filing an opposition against a granted European patent at the European Patent Office (EPO). It specifies that third parties must submit a signed notice of opposition either in writing to designated EPO offices or electronically via Online Filing 2.0. The official opposition fee must be paid simultaneously with the filing, typically through a debit order. Crucially, the challenge is restricted to specific legal grounds defined under Article 100 of the European Patent Convention, such as lack of patentability, insufficient disclosure, or added subject matter, preventing broad or arbitrary challenges to patent validity.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧获权后应建立监控机制，防范竞争对手利用异议程序拖延或无效专利。若作为异议方，需精准锁定EPC第100条 grounds，避免理由泛化被驳回。建议熟悉EPO电子提交流程，确保程序合规，以低成本高效解决专利争议，维护市场自由实施空间。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and competitors operating within the European jurisdiction, understanding these strict procedural and substantive limits is essential for effective portfolio management. Ensuring compliance with filing channels and payment timing prevents immediate rejection of oppositions. For patentees, recognizing that challenges are confined to Article 100 grounds allows for targeted defense strategies focused on technical and legal sufficiency rather than broader commercial disputes.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议是挑战已授权欧洲专利的核心行政程序", "必须基于EPC第100条特定法律理由如缺乏创造性", "需通过指定渠道提交并同步缴纳官方异议费"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Oppositions must be filed in writing or electronically using EPO Online Filing 2.0 or the Contingency Upload Service.", "The official opposition fee is due immediately upon filing and is generally paid via a specific debit order mechanism.", "Valid opposition grounds are strictly limited to those set out in Article 100 EPC, including lack of patentability and added subject matter."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立专利监控机制防范对手发起异议挑战", "精准锁定EPC第100条理由确保异议程序合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should anticipate challenges based strictly on Article 100 EPC grounds when managing post-grant risk.", "Practice note: Ensure opposition fees are paid via debit order at the exact time of electronic or written submission to avoid procedural defects.", "What to monitor: Monitor granted patents for potential opposition filings that adhere to the specific legal constraints of Article 100 EPC."]
core_points_en: Oppositions must be filed in writing or electronically using EPO Online Filing 2.0 or the Contingency Upload Service.
The official opposition fee is due immediately upon filing and is generally paid via a specific debit order mechanism.
Valid opposition grounds are strictly limited to those set out in Article 100 EPC, including lack of patentability and added subject matter.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should anticipate challenges based strictly on Article 100 EPC grounds when managing post-grant risk.
Practice note: Ensure opposition fees are paid via debit order at the exact time of electronic or written submission to avoid procedural defects.
What to monitor: Monitor granted patents for potential opposition filings that adhere to the specific legal constraints of Article 100 EPC.

items.title: Judgment of the General Court in Case T-24/25 Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO - Works 11 Michał Lubiński (Obelix)
items.title_zh: 普通法院撤销EUIPO决定：武器类“Obelix”商标应无效
items.title_en: General Court Annuls EUIPO Refusal to Invalidate Obelix Mark for Weapons and Explosives
items.summary: The General Court nullifies EUIPO’s refusal to invalidate the word mark Obelix for weapons, ammunition and explosives
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中撤销EUIPO拒绝宣告“Obelix”武器商标无效的决定。出版商Les Éditions Albert René依据在先OBELIX商标及声誉受损申请无效，EUIPO曾以声誉证据不足为由驳回。法院指出EUIPO分析有误，未综合评估商标声誉及两商标间的联系。法院强调需全局考察在先商标显著性及公众联想，认定存在损害在先商标声誉的风险，支持了权利人的主张。
items.ai_summary_en: The General Court annulled an EUIPO decision refusing to invalidate the word mark Obelix for firearms, ammunition, and explosives. The applicant, Les Éditions Albert René, relied on its earlier OBELIX mark and alleged damage to its reputation. The Court found EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation incomplete, noting it improperly disregarded evidence of use with the registered symbol and in association with Asterix. Furthermore, EUIPO failed to adequately assess the link between the marks and the risk of detriment to the earlier mark’s distinctive character or repute, despite differences in goods.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces that EU trademark offices must conduct a holistic assessment of reputation, considering cumulative evidence rather than isolated deficiencies. Rights holders asserting cross-class protection based on reputation should ensure their submissions clearly demonstrate how distinct elements contribute to overall market recognition. The case highlights the importance of properly contextualizing co-branded or associated signs when establishing independent distinctiveness and repute for specific terms within a larger franchise.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定EUIPO对在先商标声誉评估不完整，忽视关键使用证据", "强调需全局评估商标间联系，不能仅因商品差异大而否定关联", "撤销EUIPO决定，支持基于声誉受损宣告争议商标无效的诉求"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.", "Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.", "The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免注册知名文化符号，防范跨类声誉侵权法律风险", "重视商标使用证据留存，积极应对欧盟商标无效宣告挑战"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.", "Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.", "What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks."]
core_points_en: EUIPO erred by failing to comprehensively assess all relevant factors demonstrating the reputation of the earlier OBELIX mark.
Evidence of use alongside the registered symbol and in combination with Asterix was unjustifiably disregarded in the reputation analysis.
The assessment of the link between marks and potential detriment to reputation cannot rely solely on wide differences between goods.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders must present comprehensive, cumulative evidence of reputation to withstand scrutiny in invalidity proceedings involving well-known cultural icons.
Practice note: When assessing reputation, authorities must consider all relevant factors collectively; isolated insufficiencies do not negate established market recognition.
What to monitor: Future applications of this holistic reputation assessment standard in cases involving associated signs or franchise-related trademarks.

items.title: Central Division Paris accepts revocation action against expired patent
items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：过期专利撤销之诉若具法律利益仍属可受理
items.title_en: UPC Paris Confirms Admissibility of Revocation Actions Against Expired Patents
items.summary: Huntsman (Europe) BV and Huntsman Holland BV v BASF SE — Central Division Paris, 22 April 2026 (UPC_CFI_461/2025))
items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎中央分部在Huntsman诉BASF案中裁定，即使涉案专利已过期，只要原告因追索过往侵权损害赔偿而保留合法法律利益，撤销诉讼即具可受理性。法院重申在创造性评估中不受专利自身技术问题表述限制，可独立界定客观技术问题。此外，辅助请求的审查仅限于针对修改后权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动沿用对授权权利要求的异议。
items.ai_summary_en: In Huntsman v BASF, the UPC Central Division Paris ruled that a revocation action remains admissible even if the patent expired before filing, provided the claimant retains a legitimate legal interest. The Court found such interest existed because the defendant had initiated evidence preservation and announced damages claims for alleged past infringement. Additionally, the judgment clarified that the Court is not bound by the objective technical problem stated in the patent when assessing inventive step. Procedurally, it held that objections against granted claims do not automatically apply to amended claims in auxiliary requests.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕UPC程序灵活性，即便专利过期，对手仍可借撤销之诉清除障碍以索赔。应对时须精准论证法律利益缺失，并在答复辅助请求时聚焦具体修改点，避免异议范围不当扩大，强化程序抗辩策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies critical procedural boundaries for portfolio management and litigation strategy under the UPC framework. Rights holders must recognize that patent expiry does not automatically shield against revocation if past infringement liabilities are being pursued. For defendants, the ruling offers a pathway to clear patents from the register to mitigate historical damages exposure. Furthermore, the strict separation of validity attacks between granted and amended claims requires precise drafting of defenses and amendments, preventing the automatic carryover of broad invalidity arguments during proceedings.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["过期专利撤销案若为追索过往侵权赔偿，因具备法律利益仍可被受理", "法院在创造性分析中有权独立界定客观技术问题，不受专利文本束缚", "辅助请求审查限于针对修改权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动继承异议"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant faces ongoing infringement or damages claims, establishing a continuing legal interest.", "The Court may independently determine the objective technical problem for inventive step analysis, rather than relying solely on the patent's formulation.", "When reviewing auxiliary requests, the Court limits its assessment to specific invalidity attacks directed at the amended claims, excluding general objections."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕过期专利撤销风险，积极抗辩原告缺乏合法法律利益以阻断诉讼", "优化辅助请求答复策略，严格限定审查范围，防止无效理由不当扩展"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Patent owners should assess whether expired patents still pose litigation risks due to pending damages claims, as revocation actions remain viable tools for comp…", "Practice note: When responding to auxiliary requests, parties must tailor invalidity arguments specifically to the amended features, as prior objections to granted claims may not…", "What to monitor: Future UPC decisions defining the scope of 'legitimate legal interest' post-expiry and how courts independently formulate technical problems in complex inventive…"]
core_points_en: Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant faces ongoing infringement or damages claims, establishing a continuing legal interest.
The Court may independently determine the objective technical problem for inventive step analysis, rather than relying solely on the patent's formulation.
When reviewing auxiliary requests, the Court limits its assessment to specific invalidity attacks directed at the amended claims, excluding general objections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Patent owners should assess whether expired patents still pose litigation risks due to pending damages claims, as revocation actions remain viable tools for comp…
Practice note: When responding to auxiliary requests, parties must tailor invalidity arguments specifically to the amended features, as prior objections to granted claims may not…
What to monitor: Future UPC decisions defining the scope of 'legitimate legal interest' post-expiry and how courts independently formulate technical problems in complex inventive…

items.title: Mind the SEP: UKIPO signals upcoming SEP reform
items.title_zh: 英专局信号：即将推进标准必要专利改革
items.title_en: UKIPO Corporate Plan Signals Upcoming Standard Essential Patent Regulatory Reforms
items.summary: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, outlining its policy priorities for the coming…
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月11日，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布2026-2027企业计划，明确将标准必要专利（SEP）作为政策重点。针对当前生态系统存在的许可不透明、必要性不确定及争议解决成本高企等结构性挑战，UKIPO计划在2026年完成政策制定并推动监管框架变革。此举旨在提升英国创新吸引力，虽无即时立法，但预示未来将在许可透明度、SEP必要性认定及纠纷效率方面进行改革。
items.ai_summary_en: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, identifying standard essential patents (SEPs) as a critical policy priority. The plan acknowledges longstanding structural challenges within the SEP ecosystem, specifically limited licensing transparency, uncertainty regarding patent essentiality, and costly dispute resolution mechanisms that disproportionately impact small and medium-sized enterprises. While no immediate legislative changes are introduced, the UKIPO intends to complete policy development in 2026, aiming to enhance regulatory frameworks to support the UK’s position as a leading jurisdiction for innovation and technology-driven industries.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信及科技企业在英面临更严格的SEP合规环境。建议密切关注UKIPO后续政策细则，提前评估现有许可协议的透明度与必要性条款风险。应加强内部SEP管理能力，积极参与行业咨询，以应对潜在的监管变化及更高的争议解决效率要求，避免在新一轮改革中处于被动地位。
items.ai_insight_en: This update signals a sustained regulatory focus on SEP governance in the UK, aligning with broader European efforts to streamline SEP licensing and enforcement. For rights holders and implementers, the emphasis on reducing complexity and cost suggests future frameworks may prioritize procedural efficiency and clarity over substantive FRAND rate-setting. Stakeholders should prepare for potential shifts in how essentiality is verified and how disputes are mediated, ensuring internal compliance structures are adaptable to upcoming policy refinements aimed at supporting SMEs and technological growth.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO将SEP列为核心政策领域，致力于解决许可不透明及必要性不确定性问题", "计划于2026年完成政策制定，推动监管框架改革以优化争议解决机制", "改革旨在降低中小企业成本，维持英国作为全球技术创新领先司法管辖区的吸引力"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The UKIPO identifies SEPs as vital to the UK economy but cites structural issues like opaque licensing practices and essentiality uncertainty.", "Policy development will continue throughout 2026 to address inefficiencies in dispute resolution and improve overall market transparency.", "Reforms aim to maintain the UK's attractiveness for innovation without introducing immediate legal changes or specific legislative proposals yet."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UKIPO后续政策细则，评估现有许可协议合规风险", "加强内部SEP管理，积极参与咨询以应对监管变化"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and implementers must anticipate enhanced scrutiny on licensing transparency and essentiality declarations under the new framework.", "Practice note: Review current SEP management protocols to ensure readiness for potential regulatory changes focusing on dispute resolution efficiency.", "What to monitor: Watch for the UKIPO's completion of policy development work in 2026 and any subsequent consultations on regulatory adjustments."]
core_points_en: The UKIPO identifies SEPs as vital to the UK economy but cites structural issues like opaque licensing practices and essentiality uncertainty.
Policy development will continue throughout 2026 to address inefficiencies in dispute resolution and improve overall market transparency.
Reforms aim to maintain the UK's attractiveness for innovation without introducing immediate legal changes or specific legislative proposals yet.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and implementers must anticipate enhanced scrutiny on licensing transparency and essentiality declarations under the new framework.
Practice note: Review current SEP management protocols to ensure readiness for potential regulatory changes focusing on dispute resolution efficiency.
What to monitor: Watch for the UKIPO's completion of policy development work in 2026 and any subsequent consultations on regulatory adjustments.

items.title: PMAC's Case Management System now live
items.title_zh: PMAC案件管理系统正式上线
items.title_en: UPC's PMAC Launches Case Management System for Mediation and Arbitration
items.summary: As of today, 12 May 2026, the Case Management System of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) is live
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月12日，统一专利法院（UPC）宣布其专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的案件管理系统（CCMS）正式启用。该系统支持用户在线提交替代性争议解决（ADR）信息及调解程序请求。PMAC网站已设立专门页面提供系统使用指南。此举标志着UPC在优化专利纠纷非诉讼解决机制方面迈出重要一步，为当事人提供了更便捷的调解与仲裁服务渠道，有助于提高争议解决效率并降低诉讼成本。
items.ai_summary_en: As of 12 May 2026, the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) has officially launched its Case Management System (CCMS). This digital platform enables parties to submit requests for ADR information sessions and mediation proceedings directly through the Centre. The PMAC website now features a dedicated section providing practical guidance on navigating the new system. This development marks the operational availability of structured alternative dispute resolution services under the Unified Patent Court framework, offering a streamlined digital interface for initiating non-litigious patent dispute resolutions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，应关注并利用PMAC的调解与仲裁机制。相比UPC诉讼，ADR程序通常更具成本效益且保密性强。建议企业熟悉CCMS操作流程，在争议初期评估通过调解快速和解的可能性，以规避高昂的跨国诉讼风险并维护商业关系。
items.ai_insight_en: The launch of the PMAC Case Management System provides rights holders with a formalized digital channel for accessing alternative dispute resolution mechanisms within the UPC ecosystem. For in-house counsel and IP teams managing European portfolios, this infrastructure supports efficient initiation of mediation or arbitration without immediate recourse to full litigation. Monitoring the uptake and procedural rules of this system will help stakeholders evaluate its utility for resolving cross-border patent disputes efficiently.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PMAC案件管理系统于2026年5月12日正式上线运行", "支持在线提交ADR信息会话及调解程序相关请求", "官网发布专用页面提供系统操作实务指导"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The PMAC Case Management System is live as of 12 May 2026, enabling online submission of ADR requests.", "Parties can now initiate ADR information sessions and formal mediation proceedings via the new digital portal.", "Practical user guidance for the Case Management System is available on the dedicated PMAC website page."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC调解机制可降低在欧专利维权成本", "熟悉CCMS系统以便高效启动替代性争议解决程序"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Provides a structured digital pathway for initiating mediation and arbitration under the UPC framework.", "Practice note: Review the dedicated PMAC guidance page to understand submission requirements for ADR requests.", "What to monitor: Track future updates on PMAC procedural rules and case statistics to assess ADR effectiveness."]
core_points_en: The PMAC Case Management System is live as of 12 May 2026, enabling online submission of ADR requests.
Parties can now initiate ADR information sessions and formal mediation proceedings via the new digital portal.
Practical user guidance for the Case Management System is available on the dedicated PMAC website page.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Provides a structured digital pathway for initiating mediation and arbitration under the UPC framework.
Practice note: Review the dedicated PMAC guidance page to understand submission requirements for ADR requests.
What to monitor: Track future updates on PMAC procedural rules and case statistics to assess ADR effectiveness.

items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-797/23 Meta Platforms Ireland (Fair compensation)
items.title_zh: CJEU：成员国可规定新闻出版者获在线使用公平报酬
items.title_en: CJEU: Member States May Provide Fair Remuneration for Press Publishers
items.summary: Member States may make provision for publishers of press publications to be entitled to fair remuneration when they grant online service providers authorisation to use those publications
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法院在Meta诉意大利AGCOM案中裁定，成员国可立法赋予新闻出版者在授权在线服务提供者使用时获得公平报酬的权利。该权利符合欧盟法律，前提是报酬构成授权对价，且出版者可拒绝授权或免费授权。法院认为，要求平台谈判并提供数据虽限制经营自由，但有助于实现版权市场公平及保障出版业投资回收，平衡了各方权益。
items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-797/23, the Court of Justice ruled that EU law permits Member States to establish systems granting press publishers fair remuneration when authorizing online service providers to use their publications. The judgment confirms this is consistent with the Digital Single Market framework, provided the payment constitutes consideration for authorization and publishers retain the right to refuse or grant free licenses. The Court further held that obligations requiring platforms to negotiate and provide data are justified restrictions on business freedom, necessary to balance intellectual property rights with media pluralism and ensure publishers can recoup investments in a transformed digital media landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海媒体及聚合平台需关注欧盟各成员国的国内法转化，特别是意大利等先行国家。应建立合规机制，评估是否触发“公平报酬”义务，避免侵权风险。同时，利用出版者可免费授权的条款，积极争取低成本内容合作，优化内容供应链成本结构。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment validates national legislative models aiming to secure revenue streams for press publishers from digital platforms. For online service providers, particularly those operating across multiple EU jurisdictions, the decision underscores the need to assess compliance with local 'fair compensation' regimes. It clarifies that while negotiation duties restrict operational freedom, they are legally defensible if structured to support a fair marketplace. Rights holders should note the explicit preservation of their ability to grant free licenses, which remains a key contractual lever in platform negotiations.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["成员国可立法规定出版者就在线使用获公平报酬，符合欧盟法框架", "报酬须为授权对价，出版者有权拒绝授权或提供免费授权", "强制谈判义务旨在平衡经营自由与版权保护，支持出版业生存"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Member States may legislate for fair remuneration for press publishers authorizing online use by service providers.", "Remuneration must be consideration for authorization; publishers must retain rights to refuse or license freely.", "Negotiation and data provision duties are justified to balance business freedom with copyright protection objectives."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注成员国国内法转化，评估在线内容使用的报酬合规义务", "利用免费授权条款，争取低成本内容合作以优化供应链成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Online service providers must evaluate exposure to national fair remuneration schemes and prepare for mandatory negotiation frameworks.", "Practice note: Publishers should leverage the confirmed right to grant free licenses strategically during platform negotiations to maintain visibility.", "What to monitor: Implementation details of such remuneration systems in other Member States following the Italian model validated by the CJEU."]
core_points_en: Member States may legislate for fair remuneration for press publishers authorizing online use by service providers.
Remuneration must be consideration for authorization; publishers must retain rights to refuse or license freely.
Negotiation and data provision duties are justified to balance business freedom with copyright protection objectives.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Online service providers must evaluate exposure to national fair remuneration schemes and prepare for mandatory negotiation frameworks.
Practice note: Publishers should leverage the confirmed right to grant free licenses strategically during platform negotiations to maintain visibility.
What to monitor: Implementation details of such remuneration systems in other Member States following the Italian model validated by the CJEU.

items.title: La porcelaine de Limoges
items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器获欧盟首项手工艺地理标志保护
items.title_en: Limoges Porcelain Becomes First French Artisanal GI Registered Under New EU Regulation
items.summary: La porcelaine de Limoges sgurtler@inpi.fr 11 mai 2026 Avec l'entrée en vigueur du règlement européen sur les indications géographiques pour les produits artisanaux et industriels le 1er décembre 2025, la porcelaine de Limoges devient ce 15 mai la première indication géographique protégée (IGP) française artisanale et industrielle à être enregistrée au niveau européen auprès de l'Office de l'Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO). Cette reconnaissance marque une étape décisive
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月15日，利摩日瓷器成为欧盟《手工业和工业产品地理标志条例》生效后，首个在欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）注册的法国手工艺及工业地理标志（IGP）。该认证基于其自2017年起获得的法国国家地理标志地位，通过简化程序转化而来。此举赋予其在欧盟27国的广泛保护及官方标识使用权，标志着法国传统工艺知识产权保护的重要里程碑，并为其他法国手工艺品的欧盟注册开辟路径。
items.ai_summary_en: On 15 May 2026, Limoges porcelain became the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication (GI) registered at the EU level with the EUIPO. This registration follows the entry into force of the new EU regulation on GIs for craft and industrial products on 1 December 2025. Leveraging its national GI status granted by INPI in 2017, Limoges porcelain utilized a simplified transformation procedure without an opposition phase. The decision grants protection across all 27 EU member states and authorizes the use of the official 'Protected Geographical Indication' logo, marking a significant step for the protection of traditional French craftsmanship.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This registration demonstrates the operationalization of the new EU framework for craft and industrial geographical indications. For rights holders, it highlights the strategic value of securing national GI status as a foundation for streamlined EU-wide protection. The case illustrates how established regional brands can leverage existing national recognitions to achieve broader territorial coverage and enhanced enforcement capabilities within the single market.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利摩日瓷器成欧盟新规下首个注册的手工艺地理标志", "凭借法国国家GI基础，经简化程序直接转化为欧盟IGP", "获欧盟全境保护权及官方标识使用权，强化品牌壁垒"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal GI registered under the new EU regulation effective 1 December 2025.", "Registration used a simplified transformation from national INPI status, bypassing EU-level opposition procedures.", "The GI covers all production steps in Haute-Vienne, granting EU-wide protection and official logo usage rights."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟GI新规对陶瓷等手工艺品出口的合规冲击", "推动中国特色产品申请欧盟GI以提升国际品牌溢价"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders should assess if their national GIs qualify for simplified transformation to gain immediate EU-wide protection.", "Practice note: National GI recognition serves as a critical prerequisite for accessing the streamlined EU registration pathway.", "What to monitor: Future registrations of other French and European artisanal products under this new regulatory framework."]
core_points_en: Limoges porcelain is the first French artisanal GI registered under the new EU regulation effective 1 December 2025.
Registration used a simplified transformation from national INPI status, bypassing EU-level opposition procedures.
The GI covers all production steps in Haute-Vienne, granting EU-wide protection and official logo usage rights.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders should assess if their national GIs qualify for simplified transformation to gain immediate EU-wide protection.
Practice note: National GI recognition serves as a critical prerequisite for accessing the streamlined EU registration pathway.
What to monitor: Future registrations of other French and European artisanal products under this new regulatory framework.

items.title: UPC: Late filed physical evidence cannot clarify the content of an earlier filed brochure
items.title_zh: UPC裁定：迟交实物证据不得用于澄清先前提交的宣传册内容
items.title_en: UPC Vienna Division Excludes Late Physical Evidence Clarifying Prior Art Brochure
items.summary: In a recent decision from the Vienna Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC), the Court maintained EP 3705415, taking a strict...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）维也纳地方法院在Messerle诉Sabert案中维持涉案专利有效。被告Sabert试图通过迟交实物样品来澄清商业宣传册中产品特征以攻击专利新颖性。法院裁定宣传册为独立现有技术，实物证据不能用于补充或重构其披露内容，以防事后诸葛亮式解读。因该证据实质引入新的无效理由且提交过晚，法院拒绝采纳，强调证据必须基于现有论点发展而非引入新攻击。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court Vienna Local Division maintained European patent EP 3705415 in infringement proceedings between Messerle and Sabert. The defendant attempted to rely on a commercial brochure for a novelty attack but submitted physical product samples late to clarify ambiguous features depicted in the brochure's two-dimensional images. The Court rejected this evidence, ruling that the brochure constitutes stand-alone prior art. External physical evidence cannot supplement or reconstruct the document's disclosure, as this risks hindsight bias. Furthermore, the Court found the late submission effectively introduced a new line of attack based on alleged prior use rather than developing existing arguments, rendering it inadmissible under procedural rules.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC诉讼中需严格遵循举证时限与策略一致性。针对新颖性挑战，应确保书面证据本身清晰完整，避免依赖后续实物补强。若主张公开使用，须早期明确提出并单独举证，防止因混淆证据性质导致关键证据被排除，从而丧失无效机会。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC's strict adherence to procedural discipline regarding late-filed evidence and the definition of prior art disclosures. Rights holders and defendants must ensure that documentary evidence relied upon for novelty attacks is self-contained and clear at the time of filing. Attempting to cure ambiguities in written prior art with subsequent physical exhibits risks exclusion, particularly if the court perceives this as introducing a new legal ground like public prior use without proper early pleading.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院拒绝采纳迟交的实物证据，因其旨在澄清而非补充先前提交的宣传册披露内容", "宣传册作为独立现有技术，其披露范围由本领域技术人员直接明确认定，禁止事后重构", "迟交证据若实质引入全新无效攻击路径（如公开使用），将因程序不当被排除"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Physical samples submitted late cannot clarify or supplement the disclosure content of an earlier filed commercial brochure.", "Courts treat documentary prior art as stand-alone evidence, prohibiting external items from reconstructing disclosure via hindsight.", "Late-filed evidence introducing a fundamentally new attack, such as alleged prior use, is inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC对迟交证据持严格态度，企业需确保书面证据自身具备充分清晰度", "区分不同无效理由的证据提交策略，避免混合导致程序性驳回"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure prior art documents are sufficiently clear and complete when initially filed to avoid reliance on supplementary physical evidence.", "Practice note: Distinguish clearly between attacks based on documentary disclosure and those based on prior use to prevent procedural rejection of late evidence.", "What to monitor: Observe how other UPC divisions apply these admissibility standards to late-submitted evidence intended to interpret ambiguous prior art."]
core_points_en: Physical samples submitted late cannot clarify or supplement the disclosure content of an earlier filed commercial brochure.
Courts treat documentary prior art as stand-alone evidence, prohibiting external items from reconstructing disclosure via hindsight.
Late-filed evidence introducing a fundamentally new attack, such as alleged prior use, is inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure prior art documents are sufficiently clear and complete when initially filed to avoid reliance on supplementary physical evidence.
Practice note: Distinguish clearly between attacks based on documentary disclosure and those based on prior use to prevent procedural rejection of late evidence.
What to monitor: Observe how other UPC divisions apply these admissibility standards to late-submitted evidence intended to interpret ambiguous prior art.

items.title: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer): when an indefinite trade mark licence can end | Potter Clarkson
items.title_zh: 无限期商标许可非永久：英国法院裁定可凭合理通知终止
items.title_en: UK Courts Rule Indefinite Trademark Licences Are Terminable on Reasonable Notice
items.summary: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer) – An indefinite trade mark licence doesn’t necessarily last forever and the termination of a long-running commercial arrangement doesn’t necessarily require a long period of notice to be “reasonable”.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Zaha Hadid案中裁定，“无限期”商标许可不等于“永久”，允许被许可方在给予合理通知后终止合同。随后枢密院在Budweiser案中进一步指出，合理通知期的长短取决于商业背景而非仅看合作时长。该系列判决澄清了长期商业安排中默示终止权的存在，强调合同解释需符合商业逻辑，打破了无限期许可即不可解除的传统认知。
items.ai_summary_en: Recent decisions by the UK Court of Appeal and the Privy Council clarify that an 'indefinite' trademark licence is not synonymous with a perpetual one. In the Zaha Hadid case, the Court of Appeal held that such licences carry an implied right to terminate upon reasonable notice, even if the contract expressly grants termination rights to only one party. The Privy Council further guided that determining what constitutes 'reasonable' notice depends heavily on the specific commercial context rather than merely the duration of the relationship. These rulings establish that long-running commercial arrangements involving IP rights are not locked in indefinitely without clear contractual language to the contrary.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧签署长期或无限期商标许可时，切勿默认其不可撤销。建议在合同中明确约定终止条款、通知期限及违约责任，以规避法律不确定性。若面临纠纷，应重点举证商业背景的合理性，利用判例主张默示终止权，避免陷入被动锁定的商业困境。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and licensees, these judgments underscore the importance of precise drafting in long-term IP agreements. Relying on terms like 'indefinite' creates significant uncertainty regarding exit mechanisms. Parties should explicitly define termination rights, notice periods, and post-termination obligations to avoid disputes over implied contractual terms. This approach ensures clarity for portfolio management and reduces litigation risk when commercial relationships evolve or dissolve.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无限期商标许可不等同于永久许可，隐含双方无意永久绑定", "被许可方可依据默示权利，通过给予合理通知终止许可协议", "合理通知期的判定核心在于具体商业背景，而非单纯的合作年限"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence and implies a right to terminate on reasonable notice.", "The determination of reasonable notice periods relies on commercial context and business realities, not just the length of the partnership.", "Express termination clauses favoring one party do not necessarily preclude the other party from exercising an implied right to end the agreement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["签约时需明确终止机制，避免依赖模糊的无限期条款", "争议中应侧重论证商业背景以确立合理通知期的合法性"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Companies relying on indefinite IP licences must recognize they can be terminated by either party with reasonable notice, impacting long-term brand strategy.", "Practice note: Drafters should replace ambiguous 'indefinite' terms with explicit perpetual language or clearly defined termination procedures to ensure certainty.", "What to monitor: Future cases applying the 'commercial context' test to determine reasonable notice periods in diverse industry sectors and licensing models."]
core_points_en: An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence and implies a right to terminate on reasonable notice.
The determination of reasonable notice periods relies on commercial context and business realities, not just the length of the partnership.
Express termination clauses favoring one party do not necessarily preclude the other party from exercising an implied right to end the agreement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies relying on indefinite IP licences must recognize they can be terminated by either party with reasonable notice, impacting long-term brand strategy.
Practice note: Drafters should replace ambiguous 'indefinite' terms with explicit perpetual language or clearly defined termination procedures to ensure certainty.
What to monitor: Future cases applying the 'commercial context' test to determine reasonable notice periods in diverse industry sectors and licensing models.

items.title: Inside INTA London and the IP ‘carnival’
items.title_zh: INTA伦敦年会回顾：全球IP界的狂欢盛会
items.title_en: INTA Annual Meeting Returns to London: Highlights from the Global IP Community
items.summary: Against a backdrop of rooftop receptions, hot-topic debates, hectic networking, and cross-city navigation, WIPR brings you highlights and key talking points from the world’s largest IP conference.
items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权评论（WIPR）回顾了在国际商标协会（INTA）于伦敦举办的年度会议。这是该会议时隔35年重返英国首都，吸引了来自180个国家的逾万名代表参加。尽管面临交通拥堵等挑战，与会者仍积极参与屋顶招待会及社交活动。文章强调了此次会议作为全球最大IP盛会的地位，突出了伦敦作为举办地的吸引力以及业界在商标领域的高热度讨论和网络交流机会。
items.ai_summary_en: The International Trademark Association (INTA) held its annual meeting in London for the first time in over 35 years, drawing more than 10,000 delegates from 180 countries. The five-day event featured intense networking, rooftop receptions, and debates on current trademark issues. Despite logistical challenges including transport disruptions on the Elizabeth line and DLR, attendees engaged actively with the city's historic venues. The conference reinforced London's status as a key hub for global intellectual property professionals, combining high-level industry discourse with significant social interaction across Soho, Mayfair, and the City.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For European and US rights holders, this gathering serves as a critical barometer for global brand protection trends and enforcement priorities. The convergence of such a large international cohort facilitates direct exchange on cross-border compliance and emerging regulatory pressures. While specific policy outcomes are not detailed here, the scale of participation underscores the importance of maintaining active engagement with major international forums to stay aligned with evolving global IP standards and peer practices.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INTA年会时隔35年重返伦敦，吸引180国逾万名代表参会", "会议涵盖商标热点辩论与高强度社交，展现行业活力", "尽管交通受阻，伦敦地标与社交活动仍极大提升了参会体验"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["INTA's annual meeting returned to London after 35 years, attracting over 10,000 delegates from 180 countries.", "The event combined hot-topic trademark debates with extensive networking at venues across central London.", "Transport disruptions did not dampen attendee enthusiasm for the conference and associated social activities."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用顶级平台洞察欧美商标政策风向，优化海外布局", "拓展国际人脉以应对跨境侵权，提升品牌全球合规力"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Large-scale international conferences provide vital context for global brand strategy and cross-border compliance alignment.", "Practice note: Engaging with diverse jurisdictions at major events helps identify emerging enforcement trends and regulatory shifts.", "What to monitor: Future INTA announcements and panel discussions may signal changes in global trademark examination or enforcement norms."]
core_points_en: INTA's annual meeting returned to London after 35 years, attracting over 10,000 delegates from 180 countries.
The event combined hot-topic trademark debates with extensive networking at venues across central London.
Transport disruptions did not dampen attendee enthusiasm for the conference and associated social activities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Large-scale international conferences provide vital context for global brand strategy and cross-border compliance alignment.
Practice note: Engaging with diverse jurisdictions at major events helps identify emerging enforcement trends and regulatory shifts.
What to monitor: Future INTA announcements and panel discussions may signal changes in global trademark examination or enforcement norms.

items.title: HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar
items.title_zh: HP在慕尼黑墨盒专利纠纷中胜诉Ninestar
items.title_en: HP Secures Munich Court Ruling in Toner Cartridge Patent Dispute Against Ninestar Group
items.summary: In a series of lawsuits concerning various patents, Hewlett Packard Development is taking action at Munich Regional Court against several European and Chinese companies of the Ninestar Group. They all sell printer cartridges on platforms such as Amazon. According to HP, these cartridges infringe HP’s patents. After the court imposed a sales ban on CaiLuo last […] The post HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar appeared first on JUVE Patent .
items.ai_summary_zh: 惠普开发公司在慕尼黑地区法院对纳思达集团多家欧中企业发起系列专利诉讼，指控其通过亚马逊等平台销售的打印机墨盒侵犯惠普专利。法院已对其中一家名为CaiLuo的公司实施销售禁令。此案反映了跨国企业在欧洲针对替代耗材市场的严格知识产权保护行动，凸显了当地司法机构在认定专利侵权及执行禁令方面的效率与力度。
items.ai_summary_en: Hewlett Packard Development has initiated a series of patent infringement lawsuits at the Munich Regional Court against multiple entities within the Ninestar Group. The defendants, comprising European and Chinese companies, are accused of selling printer cartridges on online platforms such as Amazon that allegedly infringe HP's patents. The court has reportedly imposed sales restrictions on one specific defendant, CaiLuo, marking a significant development in this ongoing enforcement action regarding compatible consumables.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类执法动作给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the continued use of German regional courts for enforcing patent rights against suppliers of compatible printing consumables. Rights holders are actively targeting distributors across different jurisdictions who utilize major online marketplaces. Market participants should observe how these proceedings develop, particularly regarding the scope of liability for various group entities involved in the supply chain.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["惠普在慕尼黑法院起诉纳思达集团侵犯多项专利", "涉案产品为通过亚马逊销售的打印机兼容墨盒", "法院已对部分被告实施临时销售禁令"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["HP filed patent infringement suits at Munich Regional Court against several Ninestar Group companies.", "The dispute targets printer cartridges sold by defendants on e-commerce platforms like Amazon.", "The court has already imposed a sales ban on the defendant entity known as CaiLuo."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需强化德国市场专利FTO尽职调查", "应对策略应结合无效宣告与积极应诉机制"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders may leverage German litigation to enforce patents against multi-jurisdictional sellers of compatible goods.", "Practice note: Enforcement actions increasingly target specific corporate entities within larger groups operating on digital platforms.", "What to monitor: Further developments in the Munich proceedings and potential impacts on other Ninestar affiliates."]
core_points_en: HP filed patent infringement suits at Munich Regional Court against several Ninestar Group companies.
The dispute targets printer cartridges sold by defendants on e-commerce platforms like Amazon.
The court has already imposed a sales ban on the defendant entity known as CaiLuo.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders may leverage German litigation to enforce patents against multi-jurisdictional sellers of compatible goods.
Practice note: Enforcement actions increasingly target specific corporate entities within larger groups operating on digital platforms.
What to monitor: Further developments in the Munich proceedings and potential impacts on other Ninestar affiliates.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Decision On Infringement And Validity, UPC_CFI_214/2023, UPC_CFI_403/2025
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基分院：专利侵权与有效性裁决及等同原则适用
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki Applies Hague Equivalence Approach and Limits Revocation Scope in EP 3 295 663 Decision
items.summary: The Helsinki Local Division of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement proceedings involving EP 3 295 663, with AIM Sport Development AG asserting patent rights against multiple TGI Sport entities across European jurisdictions. The case included a counterclaim for revocation, requiring the court to analyze validity under Articles 54, 56, and 123(2) EPC alongside infringement claims under Article 69 EPC.
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）赫尔辛基地方分院就EP 3 295 663号专利作出侵权与有效性判决。在缺乏上诉法院先例时，法院采纳海牙分院的等同侵权判定路径，强调等同标准需累积满足，任一不成立即无侵权。针对撤销反诉，法院指出若当事人未充分论证“新增事项”，仅需回应已 substantiated 的部分。该案明确了UPC内部司法一致性的重要性及举证责任边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court's Helsinki Local Division issued a decision on infringement and validity concerning European patent EP 3 295 663, held by AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities. In the absence of Court of Appeal precedent on equivalence, the Division adopted the approach established by The Hague Local Division, emphasizing that equivalence criteria are cumulative; failure to meet any single criterion precludes a finding of infringement. Regarding the counterclaim for revocation based on added matter under Article 123(2) EPC, the Court ruled it would only address arguments substantiated by the defendant, noting that TGI provided minimal detail and did not further develop this allegation during oral proceedings.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需密切关注各地方分院对等同原则的司法倾向，尤其是海牙、赫尔辛基等关键分院。在提出无效抗辩时，必须提供详尽的技术比对与法律论证，避免仅凭形式主张导致被驳回。建议建立跨法域的专利风险预警机制，统一应诉策略以应对UPC日益增强的判例约束力。
items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the importance of judicial consistency across UPC Local Divisions, particularly regarding the doctrine of equivalents. It signals that applicants and defendants must provide robust, substantiated technical and legal arguments for invalidity defenses, as courts will not expand the scope of review beyond what is rigorously pleaded. This highlights the need for precise drafting of counterclaims and careful selection of equivalence frameworks in litigation strategy.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无上诉先例时，地方法院参照海牙分院等同判定路径以保法律确定性", "等同侵权判定标准具有累积性，任一要件缺失即认定不构成等同侵权", "法院仅对当事人提供实质论证的撤销理由进行回应，简化审理焦点"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Helsinki Local Division adopts The Hague Local Division's equivalence approach to ensure legal certainty absent Court of Appeal case law.", "Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if one criterion is unmet, no further assessment is required for a finding of non-infringement.", "Courts respond to revocation allegations only where parties present substantiated arguments, limiting scope when claims lack detailed support."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC地方分院等同原则司法倾向，统一跨法域应诉策略", "无效抗辩需提供实质技术论证，避免形式主张被法院忽略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Rights holders and defendants should align equivalence arguments with established Local Division precedents, such as those from The Hague, to enhance predictabil…", "Practice note: When filing counterclaims for revocation, ensure all grounds, including added matter, are supported by detailed technical and legal analysis to guarantee full judic…", "What to monitor: Monitor future Local Division decisions to see if the cumulative equivalence approach gains broader acceptance or if divergent interpretations emerge before Court…"]
core_points_en: Helsinki Local Division adopts The Hague Local Division's equivalence approach to ensure legal certainty absent Court of Appeal case law.
Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if one criterion is unmet, no further assessment is required for a finding of non-infringement.
Courts respond to revocation allegations only where parties present substantiated arguments, limiting scope when claims lack detailed support.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Rights holders and defendants should align equivalence arguments with established Local Division precedents, such as those from The Hague, to enhance predictabil…
Practice note: When filing counterclaims for revocation, ensure all grounds, including added matter, are supported by detailed technical and legal analysis to guarantee full judic…
What to monitor: Monitor future Local Division decisions to see if the cumulative equivalence approach gains broader acceptance or if divergent interpretations emerge before Court…

items.title: LD Düsseldorf, April 28, 2026, Procedural Order, UPC_CFI_869/2025
items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地院2026年4月程序令：关于提交文件可采性的裁决
items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf: Equality of Arms Requires Plaintiff Comment on Defendant's Late Rejoinder
items.summary: The admissibility of the documents and arguments submitted is a question of fact. Whether the defendant’s submissions in its rejoinder regarding the motion to amend are relevant...
items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）杜塞尔多夫地方分院发布程序令（案号UPC_CFI_869/2025），涉及大日本印刷诉Zapp等公司专利侵权案。法院裁定，被告在反驳中针对修改动议提交的论据是否相关及是否逾期，属事实问题，需待口头程序结束后方能确定。依据武器平等原则，法院允许原告就被告的事实陈述补充评论，作为预防性措施，且不 prejudge 最终的可采性决定。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Düsseldorf Local Division issued a procedural order in Dai Nippon Printing v. Zapp regarding the admissibility of documents submitted in the defendant's rejoinder concerning a motion to amend. The court determined that assessing the relevance and timeliness of these submissions is a factual question requiring thorough analysis, which can only be conclusively resolved at the end of oral proceedings. To uphold the principle of equality of arms, the judge rapporteur granted the plaintiff a precautionary opportunity to comment on the defendant's statement of facts. This measure is without prejudice to the final decision on whether the defendant's additional arguments will be admitted into the record.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，应高度重视程序规则的灵活性。即便对方提交材料可能逾期，法院仍可能基于公平原则给予我方回应机会。建议企业建立快速响应机制，充分利用程序赋予的评论权，同时密切关注法官对“武器平等”原则的适用尺度，以优化诉讼策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This order highlights the UPC's procedural flexibility in managing late-filed evidence under the Rules of Procedure. By deferring the final admissibility ruling while granting immediate comment rights, the court balances procedural efficiency with fair trial guarantees. Rights holders should note that 'equality of arms' may trigger additional briefing rounds even when opposing filings appear procedurally irregular, requiring agile case management during the early stages of litigation.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["被告反驳中的修改动议论据可采性属事实问题，需结合案情全面分析", "基于武器平等原则，原告获准对被告新增事实陈述进行补充评论", "最终可采性判定将推迟至口头程序结束时作出，确保程序公正"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Admissibility of late submissions in a rejoinder regarding amendment motions is treated as a complex question of fact.", "Final determination on the relevance and timeliness of such arguments is deferred until the conclusion of oral proceedings.", "Plaintiff is granted a precautionary right to comment on defendant's facts to ensure compliance with equality of arms principles."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用UPC程序规则争取补充评论机会，平衡对方突袭式举证", "关注法官对逾期证据可采性的裁量标准，完善应诉响应流程"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Courts may prioritize fair hearing rights over strict procedural deadlines, potentially extending pre-trial preparation timelines for both parties.", "Practice note: Prepare contingency responses for potential late submissions by opponents, ensuring readiness to file supplementary comments if granted by the division.", "What to monitor: How different UPC Local Divisions apply Rule 9.2 and Rule 36 RoP regarding the threshold for admitting late arguments in rejoinders."]
core_points_en: Admissibility of late submissions in a rejoinder regarding amendment motions is treated as a complex question of fact.
Final determination on the relevance and timeliness of such arguments is deferred until the conclusion of oral proceedings.
Plaintiff is granted a precautionary right to comment on defendant's facts to ensure compliance with equality of arms principles.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Courts may prioritize fair hearing rights over strict procedural deadlines, potentially extending pre-trial preparation timelines for both parties.
Practice note: Prepare contingency responses for potential late submissions by opponents, ensuring readiness to file supplementary comments if granted by the division.
What to monitor: How different UPC Local Divisions apply Rule 9.2 and Rule 36 RoP regarding the threshold for admitting late arguments in rejoinders.

items.title: Court Of Appeal, Standing Judge, April 28, 2026, Order On Request For Discretionary Review, UPC_CoA_56/2026
items.title_zh: UPC上诉庭裁定：错过期限的人为错误可获追溯延期
items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal: Human Error in Deadline Calculation Warrants Retroactive Term Extension
items.summary: The Court of Appeal examines a request for discretionary review in a patent dispute between Optopol Technology and Topcon Corporation concerning EP 2 845 534. The case involves interpretation of procedural rules governing standing and review processes under the Unified Patent Court framework.
items.ai_summary_zh: 本案系Optopol与Topcon在统一专利法院（UPC）的专利纠纷。上诉庭审查了针对EP 2845534号专利的程序性请求。法院认定，尽管代理人助理系统录入失误导致错过期限，但律所已建立核查机制，该人为错误属可原谅情形。上诉庭支持地方法院决定，允许在权利恢复请求期内隐含提交追溯期限延长申请，强调此类申请应尽早提出，但在特定未决判例背景下具有合理性。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Court of Appeal, Standing Judge, reviewed a discretionary request concerning EP 2 845 534 in Optopol Technology v. Topcon Corporation. The Court upheld the Local Division's decision to grant a retroactive term extension under Rule 9.3 RoP. Although a deadline was missed due to an assistant's data entry error, the Court found this excusable because the law firm had established verification systems. The ruling confirms that such implicit requests filed within the re-establishment of rights period are reasonable, particularly when pending case law creates uncertainty regarding procedural timing.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需建立严格的期限双重核查机制以防范人为失误。若遇不可抗力或合理疏忽，应及时利用“可原谅错误”条款申请救济。同时，注意程序请求的时效性与独立性，避免过度依赖替代性请求策略，确保合规操作以降低专利失效风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This decision clarifies the threshold for 'excusable' errors in UPC proceedings, distinguishing between systemic failures and isolated human mistakes within robust compliance frameworks. For rights holders, it validates the strategy of filing alternative procedural requests during periods of legal uncertainty. However, the Court's emphasis on timeliness reinforces that reliance on such extensions remains exceptional rather than routine practice.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC上诉庭认定代理人系统性核查下的人为失误属可原谅错误", "支持在权利恢复期限内隐含提交追溯期限延长申请的合理性", "明确依据程序规则第9.3条，追溯延期请求原则上应尽早提出"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court accepted that a human error by an assistant constituted an excusable mistake despite existing internal verification checks at the representative's firm.", "An implicit request for retroactive term extension filed as an alternative within the re-establishment of rights period was deemed procedurally acceptable.", "While Rule 9.3 RoP requires requests for retroactive term extension to be made as soon as possible, context-specific delays may be justified."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立严格期限双重核查机制，防范人为失误导致专利失效", "善用“可原谅错误”条款及时申请救济，确保程序合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Demonstrates that established internal deadline monitoring systems can mitigate risks associated with isolated administrative errors in UPC litigation.", "Practice note: Consider filing implicit alternative requests for retroactive term extension alongside re-establishment applications when procedural deadlines are at risk.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'as soon as possible' under Rule 9.3 RoP to understand how pending case law influences acceptable delay durations."]
core_points_en: The Court accepted that a human error by an assistant constituted an excusable mistake despite existing internal verification checks at the representative's firm.
An implicit request for retroactive term extension filed as an alternative within the re-establishment of rights period was deemed procedurally acceptable.
While Rule 9.3 RoP requires requests for retroactive term extension to be made as soon as possible, context-specific delays may be justified.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Demonstrates that established internal deadline monitoring systems can mitigate risks associated with isolated administrative errors in UPC litigation.
Practice note: Consider filing implicit alternative requests for retroactive term extension alongside re-establishment applications when procedural deadlines are at risk.
What to monitor: Future interpretations of 'as soon as possible' under Rule 9.3 RoP to understand how pending case law influences acceptable delay durations.

items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Order Disposing Of Provisional Measures Application, ACT_551054/2023
items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基地院：临时措施申请失去目的时予以处置
items.title_en: UPC Helsinki: Disposing of Provisional Measures Applications Devoid of Purpose Under Rule 360
items.summary: If a provisional measures application has become devoid of purpose and hence there is no longer need to adjudicate on it, the Court shall dispose of it based on R. 360 RoP...
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月29日，UPC赫尔辛基地方法院就ACT_551054/2023案发布命令。法院依据《程序规则》第360条裁定，若临时措施申请已失去目的且无需再行裁决，应予以处置。若当事人请求，法院可作出费用决定，并酌情考虑实体案件已知结果。本案涉及AIM Sport Development AG与多家TGI Sport实体关于EP 3 295 663号专利的争议。
items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Helsinki Local Division issued an order on April 29, 2026, regarding application ACT_551054/2023. The Court held that if a provisional measures application becomes devoid of purpose, it must be disposed of pursuant to Rule 360 of the Rules of Procedure. Upon request by the parties, the Court may issue a cost decision, potentially considering the known outcome of the case on the merits. This proceeding involved AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities concerning European Patent EP 3 295 663.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC临时禁令时，需密切关注程序进展。若主案胜诉或和解，应及时主张临时措施失去目的以终止程序，避免不必要的费用支出。建议结合实体判决结果，积极向法院申请费用分担，优化跨境知识产权诉讼的成本控制策略。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling clarifies the procedural mechanism for terminating provisional measures proceedings under the Unified Patent Court framework when the underlying urgency or purpose dissipates. For rights holders and defendants, understanding when an application is deemed 'devoid of purpose' is critical for managing litigation timelines and potential cost liabilities. The ability to link cost decisions to the merits outcome provides a pathway for resolving ancillary disputes efficiently once the primary legal questions are settled or mooted.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["临时措施申请若失去目的，UPC法院依R.360条应予处置", "法院可依当事人请求裁定诉讼费用，参考实体案已知结果", "本案涉及EP 3 295 663号专利，原告为AIM Sport，被告为多家公司"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Rule 360 RoP mandates disposal of provisional measures applications that have become devoid of purpose and no longer require adjudication.", "The Court may decide on costs upon party request, taking into account the known outcome of the main proceedings if available.", "The order addresses procedural efficiency in UPC local divisions when interim relief is no longer necessary due to case developments."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注临时措施目的丧失情形，及时申请终止程序", "利用实体案结果争取有利费用裁定，降低诉讼成本"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Parties should assess whether changes in factual circumstances or settlement progress render provisional measures applications obsolete to avoid unnecessary proc…", "Practice note: When requesting cost decisions after disposal under Rule 360, parties should explicitly reference any known outcomes on the merits to support their position.", "What to monitor: Future interpretations by other UPC Local Divisions regarding what constitutes an application being 'devoid of purpose' under Rule 360."]
core_points_en: Rule 360 RoP mandates disposal of provisional measures applications that have become devoid of purpose and no longer require adjudication.
The Court may decide on costs upon party request, taking into account the known outcome of the main proceedings if available.
The order addresses procedural efficiency in UPC local divisions when interim relief is no longer necessary due to case developments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Parties should assess whether changes in factual circumstances or settlement progress render provisional measures applications obsolete to avoid unnecessary proc…
Practice note: When requesting cost decisions after disposal under Rule 360, parties should explicitly reference any known outcomes on the merits to support their position.
What to monitor: Future interpretations by other UPC Local Divisions regarding what constitutes an application being 'devoid of purpose' under Rule 360.

items.title: G1/25 hearing: turning point or status quo?
items.title_zh: G1/25听证：转折点还是维持现状？
items.title_en: EPO G1/25 Hearing: Parties Oppose Description Adaptation Requirement
items.summary: G 1/25 addresses a particularly controversial feature of EPO practice: the requirement to adapt the description. The long-anticipated hearing on G 1/25 took place on 8 May 2026, and unfolded in a manner rarely seen before the Enlarged Board of Appeal – the patent proprietor and the opponent presenting somewhat aligned positions, both opposing the preliminary opinion.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局扩大上诉委员会于2026年5月8日举行G1/25听证，聚焦异议程序中权利要求修改后是否必须相应修改说明书以消除不一致。专利权人与异议人罕见地联合反对初步意见，主张EPC无此强制规定。尽管双方提出法律解释及国际协调论据，但扩委会倾向于维持要求修改说明书的现行实践。本案将决定审查与异议阶段说明书适应性的法律义务边界。
items.ai_summary_en: The Enlarged Board of Appeal held a hearing on 8 May 2026 regarding G 1/25, addressing the controversial EPO practice requiring description adaptation. Unusually, both the patent proprietor and opponent aligned against the preliminary opinion, arguing that the European Patent Convention does not mandate such adaptation. The proprietor cited the lack of explicit EPC provisions, while the opponent highlighted global harmonization concerns and Article 84 EPC implications. Despite these arguments, the Enlarged Board’s provisional view suggests it may uphold the current requirement to adapt descriptions in both prosecution and opposition proceedings to resolve inconsistencies with amended claims.
items.ai_insight_zh: 若EPO维持强制修改说明书的实践，中国申请人在欧维权成本将增加。建议企业在答复审查意见或应对异议时，提前规划说明书修改策略，确保权利要求与描述严格一致，避免因形式缺陷导致权利不稳定或额外费用。同时需关注最终裁决对全球专利实务的潜在影响。
items.ai_insight_en: This referral clarifies whether description adaptation is legally mandatory under the EPC or merely administrative practice. A confirmation of the status quo would solidify the current procedural burden on applicants and proprietors to ensure strict consistency between claims and description, impacting portfolio management strategies across examination and opposition stages.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["G1/25听证探讨异议中权利要求修改后是否需强制适配说明书", "当事人联合反对初步意见，主张EPC未规定此类强制性适应义务", "扩委会倾向维持现行实践，要求审查及异议中消除描述不一致"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Both parties opposed the preliminary opinion, arguing no EPC provision mandates adapting the description during proceedings.", "The opponent emphasized global harmonization and Article 84 EPC, noting the EPO's unique stance internationally.", "The Enlarged Board appears inclined to maintain the existing practice of requiring description adaptation for consistency."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["需强化说明书与权利要求的一致性管理以规避合规风险", "应预判程序成本上升并优化在欧专利申请与答辩策略"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Confirms if strict description-claim consistency remains a formal compliance hurdle in EPO proceedings.", "Practice note: Monitor final decision to adjust drafting and amendment strategies for potential continued adaptation requirements.", "What to monitor: The Enlarged Board's final ruling on which specific EPC articles necessitate description adaptation."]
core_points_en: Both parties opposed the preliminary opinion, arguing no EPC provision mandates adapting the description during proceedings.
The opponent emphasized global harmonization and Article 84 EPC, noting the EPO's unique stance internationally.
The Enlarged Board appears inclined to maintain the existing practice of requiring description adaptation for consistency.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Confirms if strict description-claim consistency remains a formal compliance hurdle in EPO proceedings.
Practice note: Monitor final decision to adjust drafting and amendment strategies for potential continued adaptation requirements.
What to monitor: The Enlarged Board's final ruling on which specific EPC articles necessitate description adaptation.

items.title: IPEC insights: Prosecco PDO Consortium v Prosecco International -…
items.title_zh: IPEC驳回Prosecco国际公司驳回起诉申请
items.title_en: IPEC Dismisses Strike-Out Bid in Prosecco PDO Infringement Case Against UK Spirits Firm
items.summary: The IPEC has dismissed a strike-out bid by a British spirits company accused of misusing the protected Prosecco name, allowing the case to proceed to…
items.ai_summary_zh: 意大利Prosecco PDO保护协会在英国IPEC起诉Prosecco International Ltd滥用受保护的“Prosecco”名称。被告申请驳回起诉被拒，案件将进入审判阶段。原告指控被告在不符合PDO规范的饮料、投资合作及公司名称中使用该标识，侵犯欧盟原产地名称保护法规（已纳入英国法）及英国证明商标。此案涉及管辖权、平行诉讼及PDO保护范围等关键法律问题。
items.ai_summary_en: The Intellectual Property Enterprise Court (IPEC) has dismissed a strike-out application by Prosecco International Ltd, allowing the Consorzio Di Tutela Della Denominazione Di Origine Controllata Prosecco to proceed with its infringement claim. The Consorzio alleges that the defendant misused the protected 'Prosecco' name on websites and in corporate branding for products and services not complying with the Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) specification. The case involves claims under assimilated EU regulations and the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification mark infringement. This procedural decision addresses jurisdictional issues and parallel proceedings, confirming that the matter will advance to trial.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国酒企出口英国需严格审查品牌命名，避免使用受保护地理标志。即使产品未直接销售，若网站提供投资或合伙机会并关联GI名称，亦可能构成侵权。建议提前进行FTO检索，确保营销材料符合UK assimilated law要求，规避高额诉讼风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This case highlights the continued robust protection of geographical indications in the UK post-Brexit, as EU PDO regulations have been assimilated into domestic law. Rights holders can enforce these protections alongside certification mark rights against entities using GI terms for unrelated commercial activities or non-compliant products. For market participants, this underscores the importance of ensuring that brand names and digital marketing strategies do not infringe upon established PDOs, even when offering services rather than physical goods.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["IPEC驳回被告驳回起诉申请，案件正式进入审判程序", "原告主张被告违反EU PDO法规及英国1994年商标法证明商标条款", "争议焦点包括域名使用、非合规产品标注及公司名称侵权"]

core_points_en: The IPEC rejected the defendant's bid to strike out the claim, permitting the litigation concerning alleged misuse of the Prosecco PDO to proceed to trial.
Claims rely on assimilated EU regulations protecting designations of origin and sections 10(2) and 10(3) of the UK Trade Marks Act 1994 regarding certification marks.
Alleged infringements include using PROSECCO on non-compliant drinks, investment opportunities, company names, and specific domain names like prosecco.com.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Companies must ensure brand naming and digital content avoid unauthorized use of protected geographical indications to prevent infringement liability.
Practice note: Enforcement actions may combine assimilated EU PDO regulations with national trade mark laws, including certification mark provisions under the Trade Marks Act 1994.
What to monitor: The upcoming trial outcome will clarify the scope of PDO protection regarding online activities, investment offers, and corporate naming conventions in the UK.

items.title: France Design Impact Award 2026
items.title_zh: 法国国家工业产权局公布2026年设计影响力奖40强项目
items.title_en: INPI Reveals 40 Finalists for France Design Impact Award 2026
items.summary: France Design Impact Award 2026 sgurtler@inpi.fr 07 mai 2026 Les 40 projets du prix France Design Impact award 2026 ont été officiellement révélés lors de l’inauguration de la Foire économique de Tours le 2 mai. Cette sélection 2026 met en avant des initiatives venues de toutes les régions de France et démontre le rôle concret du design face aux grands enjeux climatiques et politiques. À travers différentes scènes de vie quotidienne (se soigner, habiter, se déplacer, s’instruire, communiquer, pr
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局（INPI）于5月2日在图尔经济博览会开幕仪式上正式揭晓“2026法国设计影响力奖”的40个入围项目。该评选由经济、环境和社会三个专家陪审团共同评估，旨在展示设计在应对气候与政治挑战中的具体作用。INPI作为历史合作伙伴参与经济陪审团，强调设计作为经济转型战略杠杆的价值，涵盖产品、服务及品牌视觉等多个领域，凸显了外观设计在商业生态中的核心地位。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) officially announced the 40 finalists for the 2026 France Design Impact Award during the inauguration of the Tours Economic Fair on May 2. The selection highlights initiatives from across all French regions, demonstrating design's concrete role in addressing major climatic and political challenges. Covering daily life scenarios such as healthcare, housing, mobility, education, communication, production, and communal living, the award underscores design as a strategic lever for economic transformation. The projects span product, industrial, brand, visual identity, user interface, spatial, and service design.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视外观设计在欧盟市场的战略价值，不仅限于注册保护，更需结合ESG理念提升设计竞争力。建议关注INPI等机构的设计激励政策，通过高质量设计增强品牌溢价，并积极参与相关国际奖项以提升在欧洲市场的知名度与合规形象。
items.ai_insight_en: This initiative highlights the growing intersection between intellectual property strategy and sustainability metrics within the European design landscape. For rights holders, participation in or monitoring of such awards provides visibility into how national offices value non-traditional IP assets like service and interface design. It signals that commercial impact and ecological vision are increasingly central to official recognition frameworks.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INPI揭晓2026年设计影响力奖40强，覆盖法国全境多元设计项目", "设立经济、环境、社会三重陪审团，评估设计的综合战略价值", "INPI深度参与评审，强化外观设计在工业产权与商业转化中的作用"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Forty diverse projects were selected to demonstrate design's strategic role in economic transformation and climate response.", "Three expert juries evaluated entries based on economic, environmental, and societal criteria reflecting the French design ecosystem.", "INPI participated in the economic jury alongside partners like Bpifrance, emphasizing design's value in industrial property."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视外观设计战略价值，结合ESG理念提升欧盟市场竞争力", "关注INPI设计激励政策，通过国际奖项提升品牌知名度与合规形象"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Recognize design's expanding role in economic and ESG strategies beyond traditional aesthetic protection.", "Practice note: Monitor INPI's engagement with multi-disciplinary design categories including service and UI/UX design.", "What to monitor: Trends in how national IP offices integrate environmental and societal impact into design evaluation."]
core_points_en: Forty diverse projects were selected to demonstrate design's strategic role in economic transformation and climate response.
Three expert juries evaluated entries based on economic, environmental, and societal criteria reflecting the French design ecosystem.
INPI participated in the economic jury alongside partners like Bpifrance, emphasizing design's value in industrial property.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Recognize design's expanding role in economic and ESG strategies beyond traditional aesthetic protection.
Practice note: Monitor INPI's engagement with multi-disciplinary design categories including service and UI/UX design.
What to monitor: Trends in how national IP offices integrate environmental and societal impact into design evaluation.
